Legal Disclaimer and Confidentiality Notice 
This Request for Proposal (RFP) document (Reference No.: AILL-PROC-2025-0078) is issued by Apex 
Industrial Logistics Limited, a company incorporated under the Companies Act, 2013 and registered in India, 
with its registered office at Plot 14, Logistics Park, Bhiwandi, Maharashtra — 421 302, India (hereinafter 
referred to as "Apex" or "the Issuing Authority").
This document is issued solely for the purpose of soliciting proposals from qualified Managed Service 
Providers (MSPs) and System Integrators for the managed network transformation programme described 
herein. It does not constitute a commitment, representation, or warranty by Apex to enter into any contractual 
relationship. Apex reserves the absolute right, at its sole discretion, to:
• 
Accept or reject any or all proposals received in response to this RFP, without assigning any reason 
and without incurring any liability to any responding vendor;
• 
Cancel, modify, or re-issue this RFP at any time without prior notice;
• 
Waive any informality or irregularity in a proposal;
• 
Negotiate with one or more vendors simultaneously;
• 
Award a contract to a vendor whose proposal is not the lowest-cost submission;
• 
Conduct due diligence verification on all information submitted by responding vendors.
All information, data, network topology details, specifications, and documentation contained in this RFP are 
the proprietary intellectual property of Apex and are provided in strict confidence under the Information 
Technology Act, 2000 and the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023. By accepting and retaining this RFP, 
the recipient acknowledges and agrees to be bound by these confidentiality obligations.
NOTE: The submission of a proposal in response to this RFP does not entitle any vendor to 
reimbursement of costs incurred in the preparation of said proposal. All proposal preparation 
costs are borne exclusively by the responding vendor.
APEX INDUSTRIAL LOGISTICS LIMITED
RFP Ref: AILL-PROC-2025-0078  |  STRICTLY 
CONFIDENTIAL
Managed Network Services & Cybersecurity RFP
Page  
APEX INDUSTRIAL LOGISTICS LIMITED
SECTION 1 — Executive Summary and Introduction
1.1  About Apex Industrial Logistics Limited 
Apex Industrial Logistics Limited (hereinafter "Apex" or "the Company") is one of India's leading integrated 
logistics and heavy supply chain operators. Founded in 1994 and headquartered in Bhiwandi, Maharashtra, 
Apex operates a national network of three hundred (300) branch warehouses spanning twenty-eight (28) Indian 
states and Union Territories, with a centralised Tier-III Data Centre located in Andheri East, Mumbai.
Apex employs approximately 15,000 personnel across its operational footprint and recorded consolidated 
annual revenue of approximately INR 5,500 Crore in Financial Year 2024–25. The Company's core business 
verticals encompass bulk freight forwarding, automated warehousing, cold-chain logistics, last-mile 
distribution, and cross-border trade facilitation. Apex is a publicly listed entity on the National Stock Exchange 
(NSE: APEXIL) and is subject to SEBI listing obligations.
1.2  Current Infrastructure Challenges and Business Drivers 
Apex's current enterprise network infrastructure was architected over a decade ago and is now a material 
constraint on operational agility, application performance, and security posture. The incumbent environment 
presents the following critical and unacceptable constraints:
Pain Point
Current Impact on Operations
Legacy MPLS Network
All 300 branch warehouses are connected via legacy Multiprotocol Label Switching 
(MPLS) circuits procured from a single incumbent carrier. Bandwidth tiers are rigid 
(2 Mbps to 10 Mbps per branch), costly to upgrade, and require 60–90-day lead 
times for any configuration change.
Chronic Network 
Outages
The Company experienced an average of 4.3 unplanned network outages per 
month per region in FY2024–25, each lasting an average of 47 minutes. These 
outages directly halt warehouse management system (WMS) operations, fleet 
tracking updates, and ERP transaction processing, resulting in an estimated 
revenue impact of INR 3.8 Crore per month.
Single-Link 
Dependency
Hub warehouse sites (Tier-1) currently operate on a single MPLS link with no 
active failover path. Loss of connectivity at a hub site cascades to 8–15 dependent 
spoke sites reliant on the hub for WMS access and IoT aggregation.
No Cloud On-Ramp
Apex is executing a cloud-first transformation programme (ERP on Microsoft 
Azure, data analytics on AWS). Currently, all cloud traffic is backhauled through 
the Mumbai Data Centre, introducing latency of 180–340 ms for branch-to-cloud 
workloads and consuming disproportionate Data Centre bandwidth.
Security Perimeter 
Gaps
Branch sites lack next-generation firewall (NGFW) protection. A significant 
ransomware incident in Q3 FY2024 resulted in operational downtime of 72 hours 
across 14 warehouse sites and a forensic investigation cost of INR 1.1 Crore. Apex 
currently has no 24/7 Security Operations Centre (SOC) capability.
IoT Connectivity 
Fragmentation
Apex operates a fleet of 2.5 million IoT-enabled tracking SIMs across its vehicle 
fleet and warehouse assets. SIM management is currently distributed across three 
different Mobile Virtual Network Operators (MVNOs), resulting in fragmented 
visibility, inconsistent data plans, and inability to enforce unified MDM policies.
1.3  Programme Objectives 
Apex Industrial Logistics Limited hereby issues this Request for Proposal to solicit proposals from qualified 
Managed Service Providers and System Integrators for the complete overhaul of its enterprise network, cloud 
connectivity, and security infrastructure. The primary objectives of this programme are:
• 
Decommission all legacy MPLS circuits across 300 branch sites and replace with a fully managed 
SD-WAN overlay with active-active dual-link connectivity at hub sites.
• 
Establish direct, carrier-grade Cloud Interconnects to Microsoft Azure (primary) and Amazon Web 
Services (secondary) from the Mumbai Data Centre, eliminating cloud backhaul latency.
APEX INDUSTRIAL LOGISTICS LIMITED
RFP Ref: AILL-PROC-2025-0078  |  STRICTLY 
CONFIDENTIAL
Managed Network Services & Cybersecurity RFP
Page  
APEX INDUSTRIAL LOGISTICS LIMITED
• 
Deploy a 24/7 Managed Security Operations Centre (SOC) with comprehensive monitoring of all 
branch-edge next-generation firewalls, threat intelligence feeds, and incident response capability.
• 
Consolidate all 2.5 million fleet and asset IoT SIMs onto a single Enterprise Mobility platform with 
unified management, real-time visibility, and enforced MDM policy.
• 
Achieve a minimum of 99.99% network availability (Four Nines) at all Tier-1 hub warehouse sites on 
a monthly measurement basis.
• 
Deliver the complete programme within 18 months of contract execution, migrating all 300 sites 
across five defined phases.
• 
Ensure full compliance with the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023 (DPDP Act) for all data 
processed on or transmitted through the new network infrastructure.

2.2  Technology Environment 
Apex's current enterprise technology stack is as follows, provided for the purposes of integration planning:
• 
ERP Platform: SAP S/4HANA (hosted on Microsoft Azure — Central India region), migrated in 
FY2023.
• 
Warehouse Management System (WMS): Manhattan Associates WMS — on-premises at Mumbai 
DC, with active Azure DR replication.
• 
Fleet & Asset Tracking: Proprietary IoT platform with 2.5 million active SIMs; data aggregation via 
MQTT brokers hosted on AWS (Mumbai region).
• 
Collaboration: Microsoft 365 (Exchange Online, Teams, SharePoint) — Microsoft Azure hosted.
• 
IT Service Management: ServiceNow (ITSM) — cloud-hosted; ITIL v4 aligned.
• 
Identity: Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure AD) with on-premises Active Directory synchronisation 
via Entra Connect.
• 
Security: Checkpoint firewalls at Mumbai DC (end-of-support 2026); no unified SIEM; no SOC.
• 
Network: Tata Communications MPLS (primary); no SD-WAN; no cloud interconnect.
2.3  Regulatory and Compliance Context 
Apex's network and data infrastructure is subject to the following regulatory frameworks, all of which must be 
addressed in vendor proposals:
• 
Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023 (DPDP Act): All personal data of employees, customers, 
and fleet drivers processed across the network must comply with DPDP Act provisions including 
data localisation, consent management, and data principal rights.
• 
SEBI Cybersecurity and Cyber Resilience Framework (CSCRF) 2024: As a listed entity, Apex is 
subject to SEBI's CSCRF, mandating 24x7 SOC capability, annual VAPT, and Board-level 
cybersecurity governance.
• 
Reserve Bank of India (RBI) Payment Security Guidelines: Applicable to logistics payment and 
settlement flows processed through the network.
• 
ISO/IEC 27001:2022: Apex holds certification and requires all MSP subcontractors to maintain 
equivalent or superior certification.

SECTION 3 — Project Overview and Objectives
3.1  Programme Description 
The Apex Network Transformation Programme (hereinafter "the Programme") is a strategic, enterprise-wide 
initiative to replace the Company's legacy MPLS network infrastructure with a modern, software-defined, cloud-
native managed network and security platform. The Programme encompasses four primary workstreams 
executed in parallel under a unified programme governance structure:
• 
Workstream 1 — Managed SD-WAN Deployment: Full replacement of MPLS circuits across all 300 
branch and hub sites with a managed SD-WAN overlay, incorporating active-active dual-link 
configuration at Tier-1 hub sites.
• 
Workstream 2 — Cloud Direct Interconnect: Establishment of dedicated, carrier-grade private 
interconnects from Apex's Mumbai Data Centre directly to Microsoft Azure (ExpressRoute 
equivalent) and Amazon Web Services (AWS Direct Connect equivalent) at 10 Gbps each.
• 
Workstream 3 — Managed Security Operations Centre: Deployment and continuous 24x7x365 
monitoring of next-generation firewalls at all 300 branch edges by a certified SOC, including threat 
intelligence, SIEM correlation, and SLA-backed incident response.
• 
Workstream 4 — Enterprise IoT Mobility Platform: Consolidation of 2.5 million fleet and asset 
tracking SIMs onto a single enterprise mobility management platform with unified dashboard, eSIM 
capability, and enforced MDM policy.
3.2  Success Criteria 
Proposals will be evaluated against the following programme-level success criteria, which will be incorporated 
as contractual Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) in the resulting Master Services Agreement:

3.3  Out of Scope 
The following items are explicitly excluded from the scope of this RFP. Vendors must not include pricing for 
out-of-scope items unless explicitly invited to do so in a separate commercial section:
• 
Passive civil or structural works at warehouse sites (cabling beyond network equipment patch 
panels, rack installations beyond those required for SD-WAN CPE).
• 
End-user computing (laptops, desktops, mobile handsets) not related to the IoT fleet tracking 
platform.
• 
SAP S/4HANA or WMS application management or licensing.

SECTION 4 — Scope of Work and Services
4.1  Workstream 1: Managed SD-WAN — All 300 Sites 
The vendor must design, deploy, and operate a fully managed Software-Defined Wide Area Network (SD-
WAN) across all 300 Apex branch warehouse and hub sites. The following specifications are mandatory:
4.1.1  Site Classification and Link Requirements
Site Tier
Site Count
Primary Link
Secondary Link
Link Mode

12 sites

4.1.2  SD-WAN Platform Mandatory Capabilities
• 
Policy-based intelligent traffic steering with Application-Aware Routing for classified applications 
including SAP S/4HANA, Microsoft Teams, Manhattan WMS, and IoT MQTT traffic.
• 
Dynamic Path Selection with real-time link quality measurement (latency, jitter, packet loss) with sub-
50 ms failover between primary and secondary links.
• 
Zero-Touch Provisioning (ZTP): All CPE devices must be provisioned remotely without on-site 
engineering resources. ZTP must enable a branch go-live within 4 hours of hardware delivery on-
site.
• 
Centralised management via a vendor-provided cloud-managed controller with full Apex read-only 
portal access, displaying per-site topology, link health, and traffic analytics.
• 
BGP and OSPF routing support with automated failover; static routing where dynamic protocols are 
not feasible at Tier-3 sites.
• 
Quality of Service (QoS) with a minimum of 8 traffic classes, including guaranteed bandwidth 
allocation for WMS and SAP traffic at all Tier-1 and Tier-2 sites.
• 
Integrated next-generation firewall (NGFW) capability at the SD-WAN edge CPE, including URL 
filtering, IPS/IDS, and SSL inspection, with policy managed from the SOC (Workstream 3).
• 
Support for 1:N VPN hub-and-spoke and any-to-any mesh topologies to enable direct branch-to-
branch connectivity for IoT data aggregation.
• 
Vendor must explicitly state the OEM hardware partner(s) proposed for SD-WAN CPE (e.g., Cisco 
Catalyst SD-WAN / Meraki, Fortinet FortiGate SD-WAN, VMware VeloCloud, Palo Alto Prisma SD-
WAN). Proposals specifying 'TBD' for hardware OEM will be deemed Non-Compliant.
4.2  Workstream 2: Cloud Direct Interconnects 
Apex requires the establishment of two (2) dedicated, private, carrier-grade Cloud Interconnects from its 
primary Mumbai Data Centre (NESCO IT Park, Goregaon East) as follows:
• 
Microsoft Azure ExpressRoute: Two (2) x 10 Gbps dedicated ExpressRoute circuits to the Microsoft 
Azure Central India region (pune1 / pune2 peering locations or equivalent Mumbai-region peering). 
Circuits must be configured in an active-active ECMP load-sharing arrangement with full BGP route 
redundancy. The interconnect must support both Azure Private Peering (for SAP and WMS 
workloads) and Microsoft Peering (for Microsoft 365).
• 
Amazon Web Services Direct Connect: One (1) x 10 Gbps Dedicated Connection to the AWS 
Mumbai (ap-south-1) region via a Direct Connect Location in Mumbai (STT GDC Hua Wei Navi 
APEX INDUSTRIAL LOGISTICS LIMITED

APEX INDUSTRIAL LOGISTICS LIMITED
Mumbai or Netmagic Mumbai equivalent). The connection must support AWS Transit Gateway 
attachment for multi-VPC routing.
• 
The vendor must provide, operate, and manage the last-mile fibre and cross-connect from the Apex 
DC MMR to the respective carrier-neutral facility. Apex will provide DC rack space and power.
• 
Both interconnects must be monitored 24x7 by the vendor's NOC, with SLA-backed performance 
reporting including availability, latency, jitter, and throughput delivered monthly.
• 
The vendor must hold active AWS Direct Connect Partner and Microsoft Azure ExpressRoute 
Partner designations and must provide evidence of these designations with the proposal.
4.3  Workstream 3: 24/7 Managed Security Operations Centre (SOC) 
Apex requires a fully outsourced, 24x7x365 Managed SOC service to monitor, detect, investigate, and respond 
to cybersecurity threats across its entire network estate.
4.3.1  SOC Service Scope
• 
Continuous monitoring of all 300 SD-WAN edge NGFW devices for security events, policy violations, 
and anomalous traffic patterns.
• 
Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) — Vendor must operate a cloud-delivered 
SIEM platform (e.g., Microsoft Sentinel, Splunk, IBM QRadar, or equivalent) ingesting logs from all 
edge firewalls, the Mumbai DC, cloud interconnects, and endpoint detection agents.
• 
Threat Intelligence integration: Vendor must subscribe to at least two (2) commercial Threat 
Intelligence feeds (e.g., Recorded Future, CrowdStrike Intelligence, Mandiant) and correlate IOCs 
against Apex's network traffic in near-real-time.
• 
Vulnerability Management: Monthly automated VAPT scans across all in-scope network devices and 
quarterly manual penetration testing of the network perimeter, with findings reported to Apex CISO 
within 5 business days.
• 
Incident Response: The vendor must provide Tier-1, Tier-2, and Tier-3 analyst capability within the 
SOC, with defined escalation paths. P1 incidents must be acknowledged within 15 minutes and 
contained within 4 hours. P1 is defined as any confirmed active threat affecting more than 5 sites or 
the Mumbai DC.
• 
Root Cause Analysis (RCA): For all P1 and P2 security incidents, a formal RCA report must be 
delivered to Apex within 72 hours of incident containment, conforming to ITIL v4 Problem 
Management process.
• 
Monthly SOC Operations Report: Delivered by the 5th business day of the following month, 
containing: event volumes, MTTD, MTTR, top threats, false-positive rate, and open vulnerability 
status.
4.3.2  Data Residency for SOC
• 
All SIEM data, log archives, and security event records relating to Apex network traffic must be 
stored exclusively within India-based data centres (Tier III or above). Storage outside India is 
prohibited without explicit written authorisation from Apex CISO, consistent with DPDP Act 2023 
requirements.
• 
The vendor must provide the name, location, and third-party audit certification of the data centre(s) 
used for SIEM log storage as part of the proposal.
4.4  Workstream 4: Enterprise IoT and Mobility Connectivity 
Apex operates 2.5 million IoT SIMs across vehicle fleet trackers, warehouse asset tags, and cold-chain 
temperature sensors. The vendor must provide:
• 
A unified Enterprise Mobility Management (EMM) platform with a single dashboard enabling real-
time visibility of all 2.5 million SIMs, including active/inactive status, data consumption, location 
(where applicable), and MDM policy compliance.
• 
National Data Connectivity: Coverage across all 28 operational states with indoor and outdoor data 
coverage. The vendor must provide a coverage heatmap for all Tier-1 and Tier-2 site locations as 
part of the proposal.
• 
eSIM / eUICC Support: The platform must support eSIM remote provisioning for new device 
additions and SIM profile switching without physical SIM replacement.

• 
Custom APN (cAPN): A dedicated private APN for Apex IoT SIM traffic, ensuring data does not 
traverse the public internet and is routed directly to Apex's network via private peering or MPLS 
handoff to the SD-WAN fabric.
• 
Data Plan Flexibility: Vendors must offer pooled data plans across SIM segments (fleet, assets, cold-
chain) to enable efficient data allocation and prevent per-SIM overage charges.
• 
API Integration: The EMM platform must expose RESTful APIs for integration with Apex's proprietary 
IoT fleet management platform for automated SIM activation, deactivation, and data consumption 
reporting.

SECTION 5 — Technical and Functional Requirements
5.1  Instructions for Completion 
This section contains the definitive technical and functional requirements for the Network Transformation 
Programme. Vendors MUST respond to EVERY requirement listed in the matrix below. The following response 
codes apply:
• 
Compliant (C): The vendor's proposed solution fully satisfies the stated requirement in its standard, 
generally available form without bespoke development, workarounds, or future roadmap 
commitments.
• 
Partially Compliant (PC): The proposed solution partially satisfies the requirement. The 'Vendor 
Remarks' column MUST contain a specific description of the gap, the proposed resolution, and the 
estimated timeline to achieve full compliance.
• 
Non-Compliant (NC): The proposed solution does not satisfy the requirement. The 'Vendor Remarks' 
column MUST contain a full explanation. Proposals with more than four (4) Non-Compliant 
responses to Mandatory requirements will be disqualified.
VENDOR INSTRUCTION: Do NOT alter, re-number, or re-sequence Requirement IDs. Do NOT 
modify the column structure of the requirements table. Responses must be entered exclusively in 
the 'Vendor Response' and 'Vendor Remarks' columns. Any modification to the table structure will 
render the proposal non-evaluable.

SECTION 9 — Evaluation Criteria and Scoring Methodology
9.1  Evaluation Methodology 
Proposals will be evaluated by the Apex Technical Evaluation Committee (TEC) using a two-stage weighted 
scoring methodology:
• 
Stage 1 — Administrative and Pre-Qualification Review: Verification of mandatory pre-qualification 
criteria (Section 8.1). Pass/Fail gate. Proposals failing Stage 1 are eliminated without scoring.
• 
Stage 2 — Technical, Commercial, Security, and Reference Scoring: Scored evaluation across four 
dimensions as defined in Section 9.2.
Vendors shortlisted following Stage 2 (top 3 by score) will be invited to deliver a live technical demonstration 
and proof of concept (PoC) at Apex HQ, Bhiwandi. The PoC scenario brief will be issued no later than 5 
business days prior. PoC outcome will be weighted at 20% of the final Stage 2 score.

SECTION 10 — Pricing and Cost Proposal Requirements
10.1  Pricing Principles 
The following mandatory commercial conditions apply to all pricing submitted in response to this RFP. 
Proposals not conforming to these conditions will be deemed commercially non-compliant:
• 
Price Validity: All unit prices and commercial rates must remain firm, fixed, and binding for a 
minimum period of 180 calendar days from the Final Proposal Submission Deadline.
• 
Currency: All pricing must be denominated in Indian Rupees (INR). Pricing denominated in foreign 
currencies without an INR equivalent will not be accepted.
• 
Tax Exclusivity: All prices must be stated exclusive of GST. GST will be assessed separately at the 
applicable rate.
• 
Itemised Pricing: Bundled lump-sum prices are not acceptable. Each line item must be individually 
priced to the granularity specified in Section 10.2.
• 
OpEx Hardware Model: All SD-WAN CPE hardware, edge firewall hardware, and any other network 
equipment required for this programme must be provided under a Vendor-owned, OpEx (Hardware-
as-a-Service) model. Apex will not purchase or capitalise network hardware. Vendors proposing 
CapEx hardware models will be scored negatively on commercial evaluation.
• 
Most Favoured Nation: Apex requires a Most Favoured Nation (MFN) clause in the resulting 
contract, guaranteeing that pricing offered to Apex is no less favourable than pricing offered to any 
comparable Indian enterprise customer of the vendor.

(implementation and stabilisation) and Years 2–3 (steady-state operations). TCO must include all 
NRC and MRC components.
• 
Payment milestone schedule: Vendors must propose payment milestones linked to programme 
delivery milestones (e.g., Phase 1 completion, Phase 3 completion, Phase 5 acceptance).
• 
Volume discount tiers for IoT SIM counts (at 2.5M, 3M, and 4M SIMs).
• 
Commercial treatment for out-of-scope site additions (new warehouse openings) and for early 
termination of legacy MPLS circuits with carrier assistance.
• 
Hardware refresh commitment: Vendor must commit to a hardware refresh cycle for all SD-WAN 
CPE not exceeding 5 years from initial deployment, at no additional cost to Apex.

SECTION 11 — Terms and Conditions / Contractual Requirements
The following contractual terms will govern the Master Services Agreement (MSA) resulting from this 
procurement. Vendors must explicitly acknowledge each clause in their proposal. Proposed deviations must 
be submitted in the Deviations Register (Appendix G) with full commercial and legal justification.
11.1  Contract Term 
The initial contract term is thirty-six (36) months from the date of contract execution, with two (2) optional 
renewal periods of twelve (12) months each, exercisable at Apex's sole discretion with sixty (60) days' written 
notice prior to the end of each contract term.
DELIBERATE TRAP CLAUSE — LEGAL REVIEW REQUIRED: The 36-month minimum contract 
term is included deliberately to test vendor legal pushback. Vendors are expected to negotiate a 
break clause at month 18 and a 24-month initial term option. Vendors accepting 36 months 
without deviation may be disadvantaged in final evaluation, as inflexibility on contract term is 
considered a commercial risk by Apex. Vendors must address this in Appendix G.
11.2  Payment Terms 
Payment terms for all monthly recurring charges (MRC) and one-time non-recurring charges (NRC) shall be 
as follows:
• 
Monthly Recurring Charges: Payable within ninety (90) days of invoice date (Net-90 days from 
invoice). Invoices must be submitted on the first business day of each calendar month for the 
preceding month's services.
• 
One-Time Non-Recurring Charges: Payable within ninety (90) days of invoice date, subject to Apex's 
acceptance of the relevant programme milestone.
DELIBERATE TRAP CLAUSE — CASH FLOW RISK: Net-90 payment terms are commercially 
aggressive and are included to test vendor risk assessment. Industry standard for managed 
services is Net-30 to Net-45. Vendors who accept Net-90 without a late payment interest clause 
risk significant cash flow exposure. Vendors must propose a deviation in Appendix G, specifying 
proposed standard payment terms and a late payment interest rate consistent with the MSME 
Development Act, 2006 (where applicable) or contractual interest at the RBI repo rate plus 2%.
11.3  Liability 
• 
General Liability Cap: The vendor's total aggregate liability under the MSA for any and all claims in 
any 12-month period shall not exceed the total charges paid by Apex to the vendor in the preceding 
twelve (12) months.
• 
Data Breach and Catastrophic Failure: Notwithstanding the general liability cap, in the event of: (a) a 
confirmed data breach attributable to the vendor's negligence or wilful misconduct resulting in the 
exfiltration of personal data of more than 10,000 Apex data subjects; or (b) a complete network 
outage affecting more than 50 Tier-2 or Tier-3 sites simultaneously for a period exceeding 8 
continuous hours; the vendor shall be subject to unlimited liability with no financial cap.
• 
Consequential Loss: Subject to the unlimited liability carve-out above, neither party shall be liable to 
the other for any indirect, special, or consequential losses.
DELIBERATE TRAP CLAUSE — UNLIMITED LIABILITY: The 'unlimited liability' provision for data 
breaches and catastrophic outages is included deliberately to test vendor legal diligence. No 
commercially reasonable vendor should accept unlimited liability. Vendors must propose in 
Appendix G a negotiated super-cap (e.g., 3x annual contract value) with specific carve-outs for 
gross negligence and wilful misconduct, and cyber liability insurance coverage minimums 
consistent with SEBI CSCRF requirements.

• 
SLA credit accumulation is capped at 25% of the total monthly invoice value per calendar month. 
Accumulated credits exceeding the cap carry over to the subsequent month.
• 
Repeated P1 incidents (three or more within any rolling 90-day period) constitute a material breach, 
entitling Apex to terminate the MSA for cause with thirty (30) days' written notice without payment of 
any termination penalty.
DELIBERATE TRAP CLAUSE — PENALTY APPLIED AGAINST TOTAL CONTRACT VALUE: The P1 
penalty provision states credits are applied against the 'Total Contract Value'. This is 
commercially punitive and atypical. Market standard for managed services is to cap penalties 
against the monthly invoice value of the affected site(s), not the Total Contract Value. Vendors 
must flag this in Appendix G and propose a commercially reasonable alternative: e.g., penalties 
applied against the monthly MRC of the directly affected sites only, with a reasonable monthly 
cap.
11.5  Intellectual Property 
All deliverables, documentation, custom configuration scripts, runbooks, and management tools created 
specifically for the Apex programme under this MSA shall be the intellectual property of Apex upon full 
payment. Pre-existing vendor IP incorporated into deliverables shall remain the vendor's IP, subject to a 
perpetual, royalty-free licence to Apex to use such IP for the purposes of operating the managed services.
11.6  Governing Law and Dispute Resolution 
The MSA and all related agreements shall be governed by the laws of India. Any dispute shall first be subject 
to executive escalation (30 days), followed by mediation under the ICADR (Indian Council of Arbitration and 
Dispute Resolution) rules (60 days), and finally arbitration under the Arbitration and Conciliation Act, 1996, 
seated in Mumbai, Maharashtra.

Pain Point | Current Impact on Operations
Legacy MPLS Network | All 300 branch warehouses are connected via legacy Multiprotocol Label Switching (MPLS) circuits procured from a single incumbent carrier. Bandwidth tiers are rigid (2 Mbps to 10 Mbps per branch), costly to upgrade, and require 60–90-day lead times for any configuration change.
Chronic Network Outages | The Company experienced an average of 4.3 unplanned network outages per month per region in FY2024–25, each lasting an average of 47 minutes. These outages directly halt warehouse management system (WMS) operations, fleet tracking updates, and ERP transaction processing, resulting in an estimated revenue impact of INR 3.8 Crore per month.
Single-Link Dependency | Hub warehouse sites (Tier-1) currently operate on a single MPLS link with no active failover path. Loss of connectivity at a hub site cascades to 8–15 dependent spoke sites reliant on the hub for WMS access and IoT aggregation.
No Cloud On-Ramp | Apex is executing a cloud-first transformation programme (ERP on Microsoft Azure, data analytics on AWS). Currently, all cloud traffic is backhauled through the Mumbai Data Centre, introducing latency of 180–340 ms for branch-to-cloud workloads and consuming disproportionate Data Centre bandwidth.
Security Perimeter Gaps | Branch sites lack next-generation firewall (NGFW) protection. A significant ransomware incident in Q3 FY2024 resulted in operational downtime of 72 hours across 14 warehouse sites and a forensic investigation cost of INR 1.1 Crore. Apex currently has no 24/7 Security Operations Centre (SOC) capability.
IoT Connectivity Fragmentation | Apex operates a fleet of 2.5 million IoT-enabled tracking SIMs across its vehicle fleet and warehouse assets. SIM Management is currently distributed across three different Mobile Virtual Network Operators (MVNOs), resulting in fragmented visibility, inconsistent data plans, and inability to enforce unified MDM policies.

Head of Delivery (Project Manager) | PMP or PRINCE2 Practitioner certification mandatory; 7+ years managing large multi-site network rollouts. | Day-to-day delivery management; site migration scheduling; milestone tracking; RAID log management.
SOC Operations Lead | CISSP or CISM mandatory; 5+ years SOC operations management; demonstrable SIEM administration experience. | SOC operations governance; threat detection tuning; incident response escalation; monthly reporting.
IoT / Mobility Lead | 5+ years IoT/EMM platform management; GSMA eSIM certification preferred; telco SIM management background. | IoT SIM platform migration; EMM policy design; private APN architecture; API integration.
Evaluation Dimension | Weight | Sub-Criteria
Technical Capability & Solution Quality | 40% | • Requirements matrix compliance (TR-001 to TR-025) — 20%
• SD-WAN solution architecture quality & OEM credentials — 8%
• Cloud interconnect design & partner credentials — 6%
• Migration plan quality (phasing, ZTP, risk management) — 6%
Commercial Competitiveness | 30% | • Total Cost of Ownership (TCO) over 36-month contract term — 18%
• OpEx hardware-as-a-service pricing model quality — 6%
• Commercial flexibility (payment milestones, volume discounts) — 6%
Security, Compliance & SOC Quality | 20% | • ISO 27001, ISO 20000-1, SOC 2 Type II currency and scope — 8%
• SOC maturity: SIEM capability, analyst headcount, MT TD / MTTR evidence — 8%
• DPDP Act compliance framework & data localisation architecture — 4%
References & Track Record | 10% | • Quality and relevance of case studies (Section 8.3) — 6%
• Verified client reference calls (shortlisted vendors only) — 4%
TOTAL | 100% | Minimum qualifying score to proceed to BAFO: 65 out of 100

0 | Non-Compliant | No response provided or requirement explicitly not met

sections.title:
APEX INDUSTRIAL LOGISTICS LIMITED
RFP Ref: AILL-PROC-2025-0078  |  STRICTLY
sections.content_summary: Introduces the RFP document for Managed Network Services & Cybersecurity, including the issuing company's name, reference number, and confidentiality classification.

sections.title:
Managed Netw ork Transformation:
SD-WAN, Cloud Interconnects & Managed Security Operations
sections.content_summary: Outlines the primary focus of the RFP: network transformation initiatives involving SD-WAN, cloud interconnects, and managed security operations.

sections.title:
RFP Reference No.
AILL-PROC-2025-0078
sections.content_summary: Provides the RFP reference number and identifies the issuing authority (Group IT Infrastructure & Procurement).

sections.title:
Issue Date
10 October 2025
sections.content_summary: Specifies the official issue date of the RFP document.

sections.title:
Submission Deadline
21 November 2025, 17:00 IST
sections.content_summary: Defines the deadline for vendor proposal submissions.

sections.title:
Classification
STRICTLY CONFIDENTIAL
sections.content_summary: Establishes the confidentiality classification of the RFP document.

sections.title: STRICTLY CONFIDENTIAL — FOR ADDRESSEE VENDOR USE ONLY
sections.content_summary: Reinforces confidentiality obligations and prohibits unauthorized redistribution of the RFP content.

sections.title: Legal Disclaimer and Confidentiality Notice
sections.content_summary: Includes legal disclaimers about the RFP's non-binding nature, Apex's rights to modify or reject proposals, and confidentiality obligations under Indian law.

sections.title: SECTION 1 — Executive Summary and Introduction
sections.content_summary: Provides background on Apex Industrial Logistics Limited, including its operations, revenue, and public listing status.

sections.title: SECTION 1 — Executive Summary and Introduction > 1.2  Current Infrastructure Challenges and Business Drivers
sections.content_summary: Details the outdated network infrastructure, chronic outages, security gaps, and IoT fragmentation that necessitate the project. Highlights operational impacts like revenue loss and compliance risks.

sections.title: SECTION 1 — Executive Summary and Introduction > 1.2  Current Infrastructure Challenges and Business Drivers > Legacy MPLS Network
sections.content_summary: Describes the limitations of the legacy MPLS network, including rigid bandwidth tiers, high costs, and long lead times for upgrades.

sections.title: SECTION 1 — Executive Summary and Introduction > 1.2  Current Infrastructure Challenges and Business Drivers > Chronic Network Outages
sections.content_summary: Quantifies the frequency and financial impact of network outages on warehouse operations and ERP systems.

sections.title: SECTION 1 — Executive Summary and Introduction > 1.2  Current Infrastructure Challenges and Business Drivers > Single-Link Dependency
sections.content_summary: Explains the risks of single-link connectivity at hub sites and its cascading effects on spoke sites.

sections.title: SECTION 1 — Executive Summary and Introduction > 1.2  Current Infrastructure Challenges and Business Drivers > No Cloud On-Ramp
sections.content_summary: Outlines the latency and bandwidth issues caused by backhauling cloud traffic through the Mumbai Data Centre.

sections.title: SECTION 1 — Executive Summary and Introduction > 1.2  Current Infrastructure Challenges and Business Drivers > Security Perimeter Gaps
sections.content_summary: Details the lack of NGFW protection and the consequences of a ransomware incident, emphasizing the need for a SOC.

sections.title: SECTION 1 — Executive Summary and Introduction > 1.2  Current Infrastructure Challenges and Business Drivers > IoT Connectivity Fragmentation
sections.content_summary: Describes the inefficiencies of managing IoT SIMs across multiple MVNOs and the lack of unified MDM policies.

sections.title: SECTION 1 — Executive Summary and Introduction > 1.2  Current Infrastructure Challenges and Business Drivers > IoT Connectivity Fragmentation > 1.3  Programme Objectives
sections.content_summary: Lists the primary objectives of the RFP, including SD-WAN deployment, cloud interconnects, SOC implementation, and compliance with DPDP Act.

sections.title: SECTION 2 — Company Background and Organisation Details
sections.content_summary: Provides corporate profile details including registered name, headquarters, revenue, employee count, operational sites, and stock exchange listings.

sections.title: 2.2 Technology Environment
sections.content_summary: Describes Apex's current enterprise technology stack, including ERP, WMS, IoT platforms, collaboration tools, and network infrastructure.

sections.title: 2.3 Regulatory and Compliance Context
sections.content_summary: Lists regulatory frameworks governing Apex's operations, including DPDP Act, SEBI CSCRF, RBI guidelines, and ISO/IEC 27001 certification requirements.

sections.title: SECTION 3 — Project Overview and Objectives
sections.content_summary: Introduces the strategic initiative to modernize the company's network infrastructure.

sections.title: 3.1  Programme Description
sections.content_summary: Details the four workstreams of the Apex Network Transformation Programme, including SD-WAN, cloud interconnects, SOC, and IoT mobility.

sections.title: 3.2  Success Criteria
sections.content_summary: Lists 10 KPIs as contractual success criteria for evaluating proposals and measuring program performance.

sections.title: 3.3  Out of Scope
sections.content_summary: Explicitly excludes items like civil works, end-user devices, and legacy contract management from the RFP scope.

sections.title: SECTION 4 — Scope of Work and Services
sections.content_summary: Outlines the project scope including four workstreams: Managed SD-WAN for 300 sites, Cloud Direct Interconnects, and Enterprise IoT/Mobility Connectivity. Details site classification, link requirements, and service delivery expectations.

sections.title: 4.1  Workstream 1: Managed SD-WAN — All 300 Sites
sections.content_summary: Defines mandatory specifications for SD-WAN design, deployment, and operation.

sections.title: 4.1.1  Site Classification and Link Requirements
sections.content_summary: Specifies site tier classifications and link requirements for the SD-WAN implementation.

sections.title: SECTION 4 — Scope of Work and Services > 4.1 Workstream 1: Managed SD-WAN — All 300 Sites > 4.1.1 Site Classification and Link Requirements
sections.content_summary: Defines site tiers (Tier-1, Tier-2, Tier-3) with minimum bandwidth requirements for broadband, 4G/5G LTE, and VSAT links. Specifies active/active and active/standby configurations.

sections.title: SECTION 4 — Scope of Work and Services > 4.1 Workstream 1: Managed SD-WAN — All 300 Sites > 4.1.1 Site Classification and Link Requirements > Active / Standby
sections.content_summary: Reiterates active/active and active/standby configurations for different site tiers as part of SD-WAN link redundancy requirements.

sections.title: SECTION 4 — Scope of Work and Services > 4.1 Workstream 1: Managed SD-WAN — All 300 Sites > 4.1.2 SD-WAN Platform Mandatory Capabilities
sections.content_summary: Lists mandatory SD-WAN platform capabilities including traffic steering, dynamic path selection, ZTP, centralized management, routing protocols, QoS, NGFW, and hardware OEM requirements.

sections.title: SECTION 4 — Scope of Work and Services > 4.2 Workstream 2: Cloud Direct Interconnects
sections.content_summary: Specifies requirements for Microsoft Azure ExpressRoute and AWS Direct Connect interconnects, including bandwidth, peering configurations, and vendor partner qualifications.

sections.title: SECTION 4 — Scope of Work and Services > 4.2 Workstream 2: Cloud Direct Interconnects > APEX INDUSTRIAL LOGISTICS LIMITED
sections.content_summary: Details vendor responsibilities for last-mile fiber, SLA monitoring, and required partner certifications (AWS Direct Connect Partner, Azure ExpressRoute Partner).

sections.title: SECTION 4 — Scope of Work and Services > 4.3 Workstream 3: 24/7 Managed Security Operations Centre (SOC)
sections.content_summary: Outlines requirements for a 24/7 SOC service to monitor, detect, and respond to cybersecurity threats across the network estate.

sections.title: 4.3.1 SOC Service Scope
sections.content_summary: Outlines technical requirements for the 24/7 SOC, including continuous monitoring, SIEM platform specifications, threat intelligence integration, vulnerability management, incident response protocols, and reporting obligations.

sections.title: 4.3.2 Data Residency for SOC
sections.content_summary: Specifies data residency requirements for SOC operations, mandating storage within India's Tier III+ data centers and compliance with DPDP Act 2023 regulations.

sections.title: 4.4 Workstream 4: Enterprise IoT and Mobility Connectivity
sections.content_summary: Details technical specifications for managing 2.5 million IoT SIMs, including EMM platform requirements, national data connectivity coverage, eSIM support, and API integration for fleet management systems.

sections.title: SECTION 5 — Technical and Functional Requirements
sections.content_summary: Defines technical requirements for the Network Transformation Programme, including response instructions for vendors, a requirements matrix with mandatory specifications for SD-WAN architecture, ZTP, failover, BGP, OEM hardware, QoS, and security features.

sections.title: 5.1  Instructions for Completion
sections.content_summary: Provides vendor guidelines for responding to technical requirements, including compliance codes (Compliant/Partially Compliant/Non-Compliant) and formatting rules for the requirements matrix.

sections.title: 5.2  Requirements Matrix
sections.content_summary: Lists specific technical requirements (TR-001 to TR-007) for SD-WAN architecture, ZTP, failover, BGP, OEM hardware, QoS, and security. Includes mandatory compliance fields and vendor response instructions.

sections.title: TR-001 SD-WAN — Architecture
sections.content_summary: Requires a cloud-managed SD-WAN solution supporting 300 sites in a single management domain without hardware controllers at the Data Centre.

sections.title: TR-002 SD-WAN — ZTP
sections.content_summary: Mandates Zero-Touch Provisioning for CPE devices, ensuring branch sites are operational within 4 hours of CPE delivery without on-site engineer intervention.

sections.title: TR-003 SD-WAN — Failover
sections.content_summary: Specifies dynamic path failover under 50 milliseconds end-to-end with no manual intervention required.

sections.title: TR-004 SD-WAN — BGP
sections.content_summary: Requires BGP routing support with automated route advertisement/withdrawal and integration with Cisco ASR 1002-HX via eBGP peering.

sections.title: TR-005 SD-WAN — OEM Hardware
sections.content_summary: Mandates vendor-owned SD-WAN CPE provided as-a-Service under OpEx pricing, with OEM partner specification (e.g., Cisco, Fortinet).

sections.title: TR-006 SD-WAN — QoS
sections.content_summary: Requires support for 8 QoS traffic classes with guaranteed/max bandwidth policies, including SAP S/4HANA and WMS traffic prioritization.

sections.title: TR-007 SD-WAN — Security
sections.content_summary: Mandates integrated NGFW capabilities at the SD-WAN edge with IPS/IDS, URL filtering, DNS security, SSL inspection, and OEM firewall specification.

sections.title: SECTION 5 — Technical and Functional Requirements > 5.2  Requirements Matrix > Mandatory
sections.content_summary: Specifies mandatory technical requirements including SD-WAN portal features, project management methodology, cloud interconnect specifications, SOC availability, SIEM platform requirements, incident response SLAs, ITIL alignment, threat intelligence integration, and compliance with DPDP Act 2023.

sections.title: SECTION 5 — Technical and Functional Requirements > 5.2  Requirements Matrix
sections.content_summary: Outlines mandatory technical requirements for IoT and network services, including private APN, eSIM compliance, coverage standards, API integration, disaster recovery, ServiceNow integration, and reporting specifications.

sections.title: SECTION 6 — Project Timeline and Milestones
sections.content_summary: Describes the project timeline and milestones, including key deliverables and deadlines for implementation phases.

sections.title: SECTION 6 — Project Timeline and Milestones > 6.1  Procurement Timeline
sections.content_summary: Outlines key procurement milestones, submission deadlines, and evaluation timelines including RFP publication, vendor registration, Q&A periods, proposal deadlines, and contract execution dates.

sections.title: SECTION 6 — Project Timeline and Milestones > 6.2  Five-Phase Delivery Migration Plan
sections.content_summary: Specifies the vendor's required 5-phase migration plan for 300 sites, including technical scope, site counts, and target durations for each phase.

sections.title: SECTION 6 — Project Timeline and Milestones > 5 pilot sites + DC Months 1–3
sections.content_summary: Details the first phase of the migration plan (pilot sites and DC) with technical implementation scope and timeline.

sections.title: SECTION 6 — Project Timeline and Milestones > 12 sites Months 4–6
sections.content_summary: Describes the second phase of the migration plan (Tier-1 hub sites) with technical requirements and timeline.

sections.title: SECTION 6 — Project Timeline and Milestones > 100 sites Months 7–11
sections.content_summary: Outlines the third phase (North/West branch sites) with technical scope and timeline for 100 sites.

sections.title: SECTION 6 — Project Timeline and Milestones > 128 sites Months 12–16
sections.content_summary: Details the fourth phase (South/East branch sites) with technical requirements and timeline for 128 sites.

sections.title: SECTION 6 — Project Timeline and Milestones > Steady-State & Handover Months 17–18
sections.content_summary: Specifies the final phase (steady-state operations and handover) with technical completion criteria and documentation requirements.

sections.title: SECTION 7 — Proposal Submission Instructions
sections.content_summary: Provides mandatory submission instructions including portal requirements, deadlines, and format specifications.

sections.title: SECTION 7 — Proposal Submission Instructions > 7.1  Submission Channel
sections.content_summary: Specifies the exclusive procurement portal for proposal submission, deadline enforcement, and rejection of non-portal submissions.

sections.title: SECTION 7 — Proposal Submission Instructions > 7.2  Proposal Structure and Format Requirements
sections.content_summary: Defines strict formatting rules for proposals, including required file type (PDF/A) and rejection of non-compliant submissions.

sections.title: SECTION 7 — Proposal Submission Instructions > 7.2  Proposal Structure and Format Requirements > Requirement Specification
sections.content_summary: Specifies format requirements for proposals, including PDF/A compliance, language, page limits, and section order.

sections.title: SECTION 7 — Proposal Submission Instructions > 7.2  Proposal Structure and Format Requirements > Requirements Table
sections.content_summary: Mandates reproduction of the requirements matrix with vendor responses and remarks columns completed.

sections.title: SECTION 7 — Proposal Submission Instructions > 7.2  Proposal Structure and Format Requirements > Pricing
sections.content_summary: Requires pricing to be included in a confidential section at the end of the proposal document.

sections.title: SECTION 7 — Proposal Submission Instructions > 7.2  Proposal Structure and Format Requirements > Deviations
sections.content_summary: Specifies that deviations from RFP requirements must be documented in Appendix G with commercial justification.

sections.title: SECTION 7 — Proposal Submission Instructions > 7.2  Proposal Structure and Format Requirements > 7.3  Appendix Structure
sections.content_summary: Outlines the structure and content requirements for appendices in the proposal.

sections.title: Appendix Content Required
sections.content_summary: Lists required content for appendices including financial statements, certifications, and case studies.

sections.title: Appendix B
sections.content_summary: Specifies mandatory certifications (ISO 27001, SOC 2, etc.) required for vendor compliance.

sections.title: Appendix D
sections.content_summary: Details requirements for key personnel CVs and draft MSA/SLA schedules.

sections.title: 7.4  Clarifications and Q&A Process
sections.content_summary: Outlines the formal process for submitting RFP clarifications and handling responses.

sections.title: SECTION 8 — Vendor Qualifications and Requirements
sections.content_summary: Establishes mandatory pre-qualification criteria for vendor eligibility.

sections.title: Annual Revenue
sections.content_summary: Requires vendors to demonstrate consolidated annual revenue exceeding INR 500 Crore (USD 60M) in each of the last three financial years, with audited financial statements provided.

sections.title: ISO 27001:2022 Certification
sections.content_summary: Mandates current ISO/IEC 27001:2022 certification for vendors and subcontractors with >20% delivery scope, with certificates submitted in Appendix B.

sections.title: ISO 20000-1:2018 Certification
sections.content_summary: Requires vendors to hold ISO/IEC 20000-1:2018 certification for IT service management, demonstrating ITIL-aligned capabilities.

sections.title: SOC 2 Type II Report
sections.content_summary: Specifies a requirement for a SOC 2 Type II report (within 12 months) covering Security, Availability, and Confidentiality for cloud-managed services.

sections.title: India Operations
sections.content_summary: Demands a wholly owned/majority-controlled Indian legal entity with 24x7 NOC/SOC staffing by at least 50 India-based FTEs, plus CIN certificate submission.

sections.title: Cloud Partner Designations
sections.content_summary: Requires active Microsoft Azure ExpressRoute Partner and AWS Direct Connect Delivery Partner designations at proposal submission.

sections.title: Comparable Deployments
sections.content_summary: Mandates proof of at least two completed Managed SD-WAN deployments (100+ sites each) in India within four years for logistics, manufacturing, or BFSI sectors.

sections.title: No Material Sanctions
sections.content_summary: Prohibits vendors/subcontractors from being subject to material sanctions, debarment, or enforcement actions by the Government of India or CBI/ED.

sections.title: Key Personnel Requirements
sections.content_summary: Specifies mandatory qualifications and roles (Programme Director, Chief Network Architect, etc.) with CVs in Appendix D and restrictions on personnel substitutions.

sections.title: SECTION 8 — Vendor Qualifications and Requirements > 8.3  Case Study Format Requirements
sections.content_summary: Specifies the required format for case studies submitted as Appendix C, including mandatory fields like client name, industry vertical, programme scope, and reference contact details.

sections.title: APEX INDUSTRIAL LOGISTICS LIMITED
sections.content_summary: Contains the RFP title, reference number, and confidentiality notice, establishing the project's identity and scope.

sections.title: SECTION 9 — Evaluation Criteria and Scoring Methodology
sections.content_summary: Outlines the two-stage evaluation process, weighted scoring model, and scoring scale for proposals, including technical, commercial, security, and reference criteria.

sections.title: SECTION 10 — Pricing and Cost Proposal Requirements
sections.content_summary: Specifies the pricing and cost proposal requirements, including mandatory commercial conditions for pricing validity, currency, tax exclusivity, and itemized pricing.

sections.title: 10.1  Pricing Principles
sections.content_summary: Details mandatory pricing principles such as price validity, currency, tax exclusivity, itemized pricing, OpEx hardware model, and Most Favoured Nation (MFN) clause requirements.

sections.title: 10.2  Pricing Response Table
sections.content_summary: Provides a structured table for vendors to submit pricing details including Non-Recurring Costs (NRC), Monthly Recurring Costs (MRC), and annual costs for various services and hardware. Includes instructions for completing the table and formatting requirements.

sections.title: SECTION 10 — Pricing and Cost Proposal Requirements > 10.2  Pricing Response Table
sections.content_summary: Provides a structured pricing table for vendors to fill with cost details for network services, cybersecurity, and IoT mobility components.

sections.title: SECTION 10 — Pricing and Cost Proposal Requirements > 10.3  Commercial Narrative
sections.content_summary: Specifies requirements for vendors to submit a written commercial narrative addressing TCO, payment milestones, volume discounts, and hardware commitments.

sections.title: SECTION 11 — Terms and Conditions / Contractual Requirements
sections.content_summary: Outlines contractual terms governing the Master Services Agreement, including mandatory clause acknowledgment and deviation submission rules.

sections.title: SECTION 11 — Terms and Conditions / Contractual Requirements > 11.1  Contract Term
sections.content_summary: Defines the 36-month contract term with optional renewals, including a deliberate trap clause to test vendor legal flexibility.

sections.title: 11.2  Payment Terms
sections.content_summary: Outlines payment terms for monthly and one-time charges, including Net-90 payment requirements and a trap clause warning vendors about cash flow risks. Vendors must propose deviations in Appendix G.

sections.title: 11.3  Liability
sections.content_summary: Defines liability caps for the vendor, including unlimited liability for data breaches or catastrophic outages. Includes a trap clause urging vendors to negotiate super-caps and insurance coverage in Appendix G.

sections.title: 11.4  SLA Penalties and Service Credits
sections.content_summary: Specifies SLA penalty framework for critical and significant service incidents, including financial penalties tied to monthly invoice values. Vendors must explicitly acknowledge these terms.

sections.title: 11.4 SLA Penalties and Service Credits
sections.content_summary: Outlines SLA penalties for service outages, credit accumulation rules, and a punitive clause for repeated breaches. Includes intellectual property ownership terms and governing law/dispute resolution provisions.

sections.title: SECTION 12 — Appendices and Attachments
sections.content_summary: Specifies the structure and requirements for appendices, including site lists, compliance matrices, and deviations register. References Appendix G for contractual deviations.

sections.title: Appendix A — Site List Summary
sections.content_summary: Provides a regional breakdown of 300 operational sites with site classifications and access instructions for detailed data in the secure data room.

sections.title: Appendix B — Compliance Matrix Template
sections.content_summary: Requires vendors to complete a compliance matrix for mandatory certifications (ISO 27001, ISO 20000, SOC 2, Azure ExpressRoute) and submit evidence references.

sections.title: CM-05 AWS Direct Connect Delivery Partner Designation
sections.content_summary: Lists technical requirements for vendor certifications and OEM partnerships, including SD-WAN, firewall, and SIEM platform specifications.

sections.title: Appendix C — Pricing Rate Card Layout
sections.content_summary: Specifies the format and structure for submitting a pricing rate card, including required columns and submission instructions.

sections.title: Appendix D — Reference Matrix Template
sections.content_summary: Provides a template for vendors to list client references, including industry, deployment scope, and contact details.

sections.title: Go-Live Date
sections.content_summary: Indicates the project timeline requirement for implementation completion, though no specific date is provided.

sections.title: Services Provided
sections.content_summary: Outlines the technical services required, including SD-WAN, cloud interconnect, and cybersecurity solutions.

sections.title: Outcome Metrics
sections.content_summary: Defines performance metrics for evaluating vendor proposals, though specific criteria are not detailed in the chunk.

sections.title: Reference Contact
sections.content_summary: Requires vendors to provide contact information for client references as part of their proposal submission.

sections.title: APEX INDUSTRIAL LOGISTICS LIMITED RFP Ref: AILL-PROC-2025-0078 | STRICTLY
sections.content_summary: Closes the RFP document with administrative details, including the RFP reference, issuer, and copyright notice.

sections.title: Pain Point | Current Impact on Operations
sections.content_summary: Outlines the operational challenges caused by legacy network infrastructure, including outages, single-link dependency, cloud latency, security gaps, and IoT fragmentation.

sections.title: Attribute | Detail
sections.content_summary: Provides organizational and operational context for Apex Industrial Logistics Limited, including company size, revenue, geographic presence, and IT governance certifications.

sections.title: KPI Reference | Success Criterion | Target / Threshold
sections.content_summary: Defines technical performance metrics for network availability, failover time, cloud latency, and incident response thresholds.

sections.title: Site Tier | Site Count | Primary Link | Secondary Link | Link Mode
sections.content_summary: Specifies network architecture requirements for different site tiers, including bandwidth, link types, and redundancy configurations.

sections.title: Req. ID | Category | Description | Priority | Vendor Response | Vendor Remarks
sections.content_summary: Lists mandatory technical requirements for SD-WAN implementation, including architecture, zero-touch provisioning, failover, and BGP integration.

sections.title: Procurement Milestone | Target Date | Notes
sections.content_summary: Outlines key procurement timeline milestones including RFP publication, vendor registration deadlines, pre-bid conference, Q&A submission, proposal deadlines, and contract execution dates.

sections.title: Phase | Title | Scope | Site Count | Target Duration
sections.content_summary: Describes the project's phased implementation plan across five phases, detailing technical objectives, site coverage, and timeline for SD-WAN deployment and system integration.

sections.title: Phase | Description | Activities | Sites | Timeline
sections.content_summary: Continues the project scope description with additional implementation phases covering branch site rollouts, steady-state operations, and final handover requirements.

sections.title: Requirement | Specification
sections.content_summary: Specifies mandatory proposal formatting rules including file format, language, page limits, section ordering, requirements matrix completion, pricing placement, and deviation documentation requirements.

sections.title: Appendix | Content Required
sections.content_summary: Lists required appendices for vendor submissions including audited financial statements, compliance certifications (ISO 27001, SOC 2, etc.), and enterprise case studies with specific formatting requirements.

sections.title: Client Name | Industry | Sites Deployed | Go-Live Date | Services Provided | Outcome Metrics | Reference Contact
sections.content_summary: Requires vendors to provide references/case studies with client details, services delivered, and outcome metrics. Structured as a fillable table for vendor responses.

sections.title: CM-ID | Requirement | Compliance | Reference | Vendor Response
sections.content_summary: Lists mandatory compliance requirements including legal entity registration, data localization, OEM partnerships, and certification standards. Vendors must indicate compliance status and provide responses.

red_flags: Potential conflict between RFP's SLA penalty requirements and POL-534 liability cap
Uncertainty about Azure ExpressRoute support in POL-412's 'private Hyperscaler pipes'
go_no_go_result.justification: Company capabilities align with core RFP requirements for SD-WAN, cloud interconnects, and managed security. Data localization and certification requirements are satisfied. Minor risks exist around SLA liability caps and Azure ExpressRoute specifics.

requirement_mappings.reasoning: Company provides 350+ Node SD-WAN solution with ZTP explicitly stated in policy
Active-Active SD-WAN fabrics explicitly mentioned in network transformation policy
Company enforces 100% data localization and maintains required security certifications

requirement_mappings.requirement_text: 24/7 SOC availability with cloud SIEM ingestion
requirement_mappings.reasoning: Cloud SIEM ingestion capabilities explicitly stated in SD-WAN solution policy
Policy mentions 'private Hyperscaler pipes' but doesn't specifically name Azure ExpressRoute

requirement_mappings.requirement_text: SLA penalties for network downtime exceeding 99.99%
requirement_mappings.reasoning: Company liability capped at 100% of fees - may conflict with RFP's SLA penalty structure

requirement_mappings.requirement_text: Data residency in India for all SD-WAN configurations
requirement_mappings.reasoning: 100% data localization enforced for SD-WAN operations in India

requirements.text: The SD-WAN solution MUST be a cloud-managed, fully software-defined overlay capable of supporting all 300 Apex branch sites within a single management domain without requiring a hardware controller at the Data Centre.
requirements.source_section:
APEX INDUSTRIAL LOGISTICS LIMITED
RFP Ref: AILL-PROC-2025-0078  |  STRICTLY

requirements.text: Zero-Touch Provisioning (ZTP) MUST be supported for all CPE devices. A branch site MUST be fully operational within 4 hours of CPE delivery, without requiring an on-site engineer from the vendor.
requirements.source_section:
APEX INDUSTRIAL LOGISTICS LIMITED
RFP Ref: AILL-PROC-2025-0078  |  STRICTLY

requirements.text: Dynamic path failover from primary to secondary link MUST complete in under 50 milliseconds as measured end-to-end from packet loss detection to traffic rerouting, without manual intervention.
requirements.source_section:
APEX INDUSTRIAL LOGISTICS LIMITED
RFP Ref: AILL-PROC-2025-0078  |  STRICTLY

requirements.text: The solution MUST support BGP routing with automated route advertisement and withdrawal upon link failure, supporting integration with Apex's Mumbai DC edge router (Cisco ASR 1002-HX) via eBGP peering.
requirements.source_section:
APEX INDUSTRIAL LOGISTICS LIMITED
RFP Ref: AILL-PROC-2025-0078  |  STRICTLY

requirements.text: Vendor MUST specify the OEM hardware partner(s) for SD-WAN CPE (e.g., Cisco, Fortinet, VMware Velocloud, Palo Alto). CPE must be provided as-a-Service under OpEx pricing. Vendor-owned hardware is mandatory; Apex will not purchase CPE outright.
requirements.source_section:
APEX INDUSTRIAL LOGISTICS LIMITED
RFP Ref: AILL-PROC-2025-0078  |  STRICTLY

requirements.text: The solution MUST support a minimum of 8 QoS traffic classes with guaranteed bandwidth (GBW) and maximum bandwidth (MBW) policies configurable per application, per site. Guaranteed bandwidth for SAP S/4HANA and WMS traffic must be configurable independently of other traffic classes.
requirements.source_section:

RFP Ref: AILL-PROC-2025-0078  |  STRICTLY

requirements.text: Integrated NGFW capability at the SD-WAN edge MUST include IPS/IDS, URL filtering (minimum 60 URL categories), DNS security, and SSL deep inspection. Vendor must specify the OEM firewall solution (e.g., Fortinet FortiGate, Palo Alto Networks NGFW, Check Point).
requirements.source_section:
APEX INDUSTRIAL LOGISTICS LIMITED
RFP Ref: AILL-PROC-2025-0078  |  STRICTLY

requirements.text: A self-service portal with role-based access control (RBAC) MUST be provided to Apex IT staff with read-only visibility of network topology, link health, traffic statistics, and active alerts across all 300 sites. No additional licensing cost for portal access.

requirements.text: Vendor MUST provide a detailed 300-site migration project plan conforming to either PRINCE2 or PMBOK methodology. A dedicated Project Manager (minimum PMP or PRINCE2 Practitioner certified) must be named in the proposal and committed to the programme through completion.

requirements.text: All SD-WAN CPE hardware, edge firewall hardware, and any other network equipment required for this programme must be provided under a Vendor-owned, OpEx (Hardware-as-a-Service) model.

TCO must include all NRC and MRC components.

validated_requirements.text: The SD-WAN solution MUST be a cloud-managed, fully software-defined overlay capable of supporting all 300 Apex branch sites within a single management domain without requiring a hardware controller at the Data Centre.
validated_requirements.source_section:
APEX INDUSTRIAL LOGISTICS LIMITED
RFP Ref: AILL-PROC-2025-0078  |  STRICTLY

validated_requirements.text: Zero-Touch Provisioning (ZTP) MUST be supported for all CPE devices. A branch site MUST be fully operational within 4 hours of CPE delivery, without requiring an on-site engineer from the vendor.
validated_requirements.source_section:
APEX INDUSTRIAL LOGISTICS LIMITED
RFP Ref: AILL-PROC-2025-0078  |  STRICTLY

validated_requirements.text: Dynamic path failover from primary to secondary link MUST complete in under 50 milliseconds as measured end-to-end from packet loss detection to traffic rerouting, without manual intervention.
validated_requirements.source_section:
APEX INDUSTRIAL LOGISTICS LIMITED
RFP Ref: AILL-PROC-2025-0078  |  STRICTLY

validated_requirements.text: The solution MUST support BGP routing with automated route advertisement and withdrawal upon link failure, supporting integration with Apex's Mumbai DC edge router (Cisco ASR 1002-HX) via eBGP peering.
validated_requirements.source_section:
APEX INDUSTRIAL LOGISTICS LIMITED
RFP Ref: AILL-PROC-2025-0078  |  STRICTLY

validated_requirements.text: Vendor MUST specify the OEM hardware partner(s) for SD-WAN CPE (e.g., Cisco, Fortinet, VMware Velocloud, Palo Alto). CPE must be provided as-a-Service under OpEx pricing. Vendor-owned hardware is mandatory; Apex will not purchase CPE outright.
validated_requirements.source_section:
APEX INDUSTRIAL LOGISTICS LIMITED
RFP Ref: AILL-PROC-2025-0078  |  STRICTLY

validated_requirements.text: The solution MUST support a minimum of 8 QoS traffic classes with guaranteed bandwidth (GBW) and maximum bandwidth (MBW) policies configurable per application, per site. Guaranteed bandwidth for SAP S/4HANA and WMS traffic must be configurable independently of other traffic classes.
validated_requirements.source_section:
APEX INDUSTRIAL LOGISTICS LIMITED
RFP Ref: AILL-PROC-2025-0078  |  STRICTLY

validated_requirements.text: Integrated NGFW capability at the SD-WAN edge MUST include IPS/IDS, URL filtering (minimum 60 URL categories), DNS security, and SSL deep inspection. Vendor must specify the OEM firewall solution (e.g., Fortinet FortiGate, Palo Alto Networks NGFW, Check Point).
validated_requirements.source_section:
APEX INDUSTRIAL LOGISTICS LIMITED
RFP Ref: AILL-PROC-2025-0078  |  STRICTLY

validated_requirements.text: A self-service portal with role-based access control (RBAC) MUST be provided to Apex IT staff with read-only visibility of network topology, link health, traffic statistics, and active alerts across all 300 sites. No additional licensing cost for portal access.
validated_requirements.source_section:
APEX INDUSTRIAL LOGISTICS LIMITED
RFP Ref: AILL-PROC-2025-0078  |  STRICTLY

validated_requirements.text: Vendor MUST provide a detailed 300-site migration project plan conforming to either PRINCE2 or PMBOK methodology. A dedicated Project Manager (minimum PMP or PRINCE2 Practitioner certified) must be named in the proposal and committed to the programme through completion.
validated_requirements.source_section:
APEX INDUSTRIAL LOGISTICS LIMITED
RFP Ref: AILL-PROC-2025-0078  |  STRICTLY

validated_requirements.text: Vendor MUST hold an active Microsoft Azure ExpressRoute Partner designation and provide two (2) x 10 Gbps circuits in active-active configuration to the Azure Central India region. Evidence of Microsoft partner designation MUST be attached as Appendix B.
validated_requirements.source_section:
APEX INDUSTRIAL LOGISTICS LIMITED
RFP Ref: AILL-PROC-2025-0078  |  STRICTLY

validated_requirements.text: Vendor MUST hold an active AWS Direct Connect Delivery Partner designation and provide one (1) x 10 Gbps Dedicated Connection to the AWS ap-south-1 Mumbai region. Evidence of AWS partner designation MUST be attached as Appendix B.
validated_requirements.source_section:
APEX INDUSTRIAL LOGISTICS LIMITED
RFP Ref: AILL-PROC-2025-0078  |  STRICTLY

validated_requirements.text: The Azure ExpressRoute interconnect MUST achieve a maximum round-trip latency of 8 ms between the Apex Mumbai DC and the Azure Central India region under steady-state traffic conditions. Vendor must provide benchmark test evidence from equivalent deployments.
validated_requirements.source_section:
APEX INDUSTRIAL LOGISTICS LIMITED
RFP Ref: AILL-PROC-2025-0078  |  STRICTLY

validated_requirements.text: The Managed SOC service MUST operate 24 hours per day, 7 days per week, 365 days per year (including Indian public holidays) with no scheduled maintenance windows that reduce monitoring coverage below Tier-1 analyst staffing levels.
validated_requirements.source_section:
APEX INDUSTRIAL LOGISTICS LIMITED
RFP Ref: AILL-PROC-2025-0078  |  STRICTLY

validated_requirements.text: The SIEM platform used by the SOC MUST ingest logs from all 300 SD-WAN edge devices, the Mumbai DC, and cloud interconnects. The vendor must name the SIEM product proposed (e.g., Microsoft Sentinel, Splunk Enterprise Security, IBM QRadar, or equivalent). SIEM data MUST be stored in India.
validated_requirements.source_section:
APEX INDUSTRIAL LOGISTICS LIMITED
RFP Ref: AILL-PROC-2025-0078  |  STRICTLY

validated_requirements.text: P1 incidents MUST be acknowledged within 15 minutes and contained within 4 hours. P2 incidents MUST be acknowledged within 30 minutes and resolved within 8 hours. P3 incidents within 2 hours acknowledgement and 24 hours resolution. All timings measured 24x7.
validated_requirements.source_section:
APEX INDUSTRIAL LOGISTICS LIMITED
RFP Ref: AILL-PROC-2025-0078  |  STRICTLY

validated_requirements.text: The SOC MUST operate Incident Management, Problem Management, and Change Management processes conforming to ITIL v4. Root Cause Analysis (RCA) reports for P1 and P2 incidents MUST be delivered within 72 hours of incident containment. Vendor must provide sample RCA template with proposal.
validated_requirements.source_section:
APEX INDUSTRIAL LOGISTICS LIMITED
RFP Ref: AILL-PROC-2025-0078  |  STRICTLY

validated_requirements.text: Vendor MUST demonstrate active subscriptions to a minimum of two (2) commercial Threat Intelligence platforms and provide evidence of integration with the proposed SIEM, including IOC ingestion frequency and automated blocking workflow.

TR-006 mentions 'guaranteed bandwidth (GBW) and maximum bandwidth (MBW) policies configurable per application, per site' which could be clearer
While not exact duplicates, both TR-008 and TR-014 relate to visibility and monitoring, consider consolidation
No direct contradictions found between requirements
TR-012 mentions '8 ms' latency which matches KPI-04 < 8 ms round-trip, no error found
TR-015 P1 incidents acknowledgement within 15 minutes and contained within 4 hours matches the RFP
Requirement uses 'All 300 sites' without specifying if it's for all sites or a specific subset
Requirement says '≥ 99.95% monthly' but RFP says '≥ 99.95% monthly' which matches, however some KPIs are not directly cross-referenced
Similar requirements for different site types (Tier-1, Tier-2, Tier-3) could be consolidated
Requirements use 'named' without specifying what 'named' entails
Requirement says ≥ 99.95% which matches RFP source
Requirement seems complete but verify against original RFP
Some requirements lack specific details or measurable criteria
Some requirements use imprecise language without measurable criteria
REQ-007 and REQ-013 both require ISO 27001 certification
Requirement uses 'API integration' without defining specific details
Requirement 'P1 | Critical Network Outage' seems incomplete
Requirement says '18 months' but seems correct based on RFP, needs verification
Requirement 'eSIM / eUICC Support' seems incomplete, lacks measurable criteria
Requirements lack specific context or details
Requirement uses 'within 72 hours' without specifying business hours or timezone
No apparent duplicates
No apparent contradictions
Requirements use general terms like 'milestone tracking' and 'RAID log management' without defining specific measurable criteria.
REQ-0069 and REQ-0070 both describe operational requirements for SOC lead personnel with similar keywords.
REQ-0089 mentions SLA credit accumulation capped at 25% but does not accurately reflect original RFP text which mentions specific invoice and calendar month conditions.
REQ-0075 appears truncated; original RFP text may contain additional details about SOC services provided.
Requirement uses 'commercially reasonable alternative' and 'reasonable monthly cap' without defining measurable criteria
No factual error found but requirement seems out of context with provided source section
