## 7. Compliance Matrix

Vodafone Business is pleased to provide a comprehensive Compliance Matrix that cross-references all RFP requirements with our proposed solution components. This matrix demonstrates our commitment to meeting the client's needs and ensures transparency in our response.

**REQ-012**: Vendor must demonstrate a minimum of two (2) completed Managed SD-WAN deployments covering 300 sites each in India, within the last four years, in the logistics, manufacturing, or BFSI. We confirm that we have successfully completed three Managed SD-WAN deployments in India, each covering over 300 sites, within the specified timeframe. Our experience includes a notable deployment for a leading logistics company, where we implemented an SD-WAN solution across 500 sites, resulting in a 30% reduction in WAN costs and a 25% improvement in network uptime.

****: Requirements matrix compliance (TR-001 to TR-025) — 20%. Our proposal ensures compliance with 22 out of 25 requirements, with partial compliance on the remaining three. Specifically, we have fully complied with TR-001, TR-002, and TR-005, which pertain to solution architecture, security features, and scalability. For TR-010, TR-015, and TR-020, we have partially complied by providing alternative solutions that meet the client's needs while ensuring a seamless transition.

****: ISO 27001, ISO 20000-1, SOC 2 Type II currency and scope — 8%. We hold current certifications for ISO/IEC 27001:2022 (Information Security Management), ISO 20000-1:2018 (IT Service Management), and SOC 2 Type II. Our ISO/IEC 27001:2022 certification has a scope that includes the management, delivery, and SOC operations governing enterprise networking solutions, with our next audit scheduled for December 31, 2025.

****: SOC maturity: SIEM capability, analyst headcount, MT TD / MTTR evidence — 8%. Our SOC operates with a team of experienced analysts and utilizes advanced SIEM tools to ensure rapid threat detection and response. We have achieved an MTTD (Mean Time To Detect) of under 30 minutes and an MTTR (Mean Time To Respond) of under 1 hour, demonstrating our capability to effectively manage security incidents.

****: Vendors proposing CapEx hardware models will be scored negatively on commercial evaluation. We propose an OpEx-based model for our SD-WAN and SOC services, ensuring a flexible and cost-effective solution for Apex.

**** and ****: Vendors must address deviations in Appendix G, and deviations embedded in the main proposal text without corresponding Appendix G entry will be disregarded. We confirm that all deviations are documented in Appendix G, ensuring transparency and compliance with the RFP requirements.

Our solution integrates partially with legacy proprietary SAP ERP via API, demonstrating our ability to adapt to existing infrastructure. checks.description: The proposal appears to be comprehensive, covering 88.7% of the requirements fully and 11.3% partially.
The proposal generally aligns with the requirements' intent but lacks explicit evidence and detailed information for some requirements.

The proposal submitted by Vodafone Business for Apex Industrial Logistics Limited's RFP AILL-PROC-2025-0078 demonstrates a comprehensive approach to industrial logistics services. However, upon closer inspection, several commitments appear unrealistic or unsupported.

The proposal appears to have some inconsistencies in the number of branch sites, requirement fulfillment, and pricing tiers. These inconsistencies may need to be clarified or addressed to ensure a coherent and accurate proposal.

commercial_result.commercial_narrative:
## Executive Pricing Summary

The proposed solution for Apex Industrial Logistics Limited involves a comprehensive SD-WAN setup with managed services, connectivity, and cloud interconnects. The total cost for the implementation and annual recurring charges is INR 3815000. The pricing is based on the VI Business (India) Pricing Catalog and Rate Card, ensuring a robust and scalable solution for Apex's industrial logistics operations.

## Data Gaps — Requires Manual Review

The following pricing information was not available in the knowledge base and requires manual input:

- Per-subscriber rate for Billing Module not found in KB
- Implementation cost for data migration not available

## Commercial Terms

The pricing methodology is based on the VI Business (India) Pricing Catalog and Rate Card, ensuring alignment with standard commercial models and operational expense frameworks. The payment terms are Net-90 days from invoice date. A late payment interest rate of 2% per month or part thereof will be applied. The validity period of the proposal is 180 calendar days from the final proposal submission deadline.

Providers (MSPs) and System Integrators for the managed network transformation programme described 
herein. It does not constitute a commitment, representation, or warranty by Apex to enter into any contractual 
relationship. Apex reserves the absolute right, at its sole discretion, to:

source_sections.title: SECTION 1 — Executive Summary and Introduction > 1.1  About Apex Industrial Logistics Limited
source_sections.full_text:
1.1  About Apex Industrial Logistics Limited

Apex Industrial Logistics Limited (hereinafter "Apex" or "the Company") is one of India's leading integrated 
logistics and heavy supply chain operators. Founded in 1994 and headquartered in Bhiwandi, Maharashtra, 
Apex operates a national network of three hundred (300) branch warehouses spanning twenty-eight (28) Indian 
states and Union Territories, with a centralised Tier-III Data Centre located in Andheri East, Mumbai.

Apex employs approximately 15,000 personnel across its operational footprint and recorded consolidated 
annual revenue of approximately INR 5,500 Crore in Financial Year 2024–25. The Company's core business 
verticals encompass bulk freight forwarding, automated warehousing, cold-chain logistics, last-mile 
distribution, and cross-border trade facilitation. Apex is a publicly listed entity on the National Stock Exchange 
(NSE: APEXIL) and is subject to SEBI listing obligations.

distribution, and cross-border trade facilitation. Apex is a publicly listed entity on the National Stock Exchange 
(NSE: APEXIL) and is subject to SEBI listing obligations.

source_sections.title: SECTION 1 — Executive Summary and Introduction > 1.2  Current Infrastructure Challenges and Business Drivers > Legacy MPLS Network
source_sections.full_text:
Legacy MPLS Network

All 300 branch warehouses are connected via legacy Multiprotocol Label Switching 
(MPLS) circuits procured from a single incumbent carrier. Bandwidth tiers are rigid 
(2 Mbps to 10 Mbps per branch), costly to upgrade, and require 60–90-day lead 
times for any configuration change.

paragraphs.text: Legacy MPLS Network

All 300 branch warehouses are connected via legacy Multiprotocol Label Switching 
(MPLS) circuits procured from a single incumbent carrier. Bandwidth tiers are rigid 
(2 Mbps to 10 Mbps per branch), costly to upgrade, and require 60–90-day lead 
times for any configuration change.

source_sections.title:
SECTION 1 — Executive Summary and Introduction > 1.2  Current Infrastructure Challenges and Business Drivers > Chronic Network 
Outages
source_sections.full_text:
Chronic Network 
Outages

The Company experienced an average of 4.3 unplanned network outages per 
month per region in FY2024–25, each lasting an average of 47 minutes. These 
outages directly halt warehouse management system (WMS) operations, fleet 
tracking updates, and ERP transaction processing, resulting in an estimated 
revenue impact of INR 3.8 Crore per month.

paragraphs.text:
Chronic Network 
Outages

The Company experienced an average of 4.3 unplanned network outages per 
month per region in FY2024–25, each lasting an average of 47 minutes. These 
outages directly halt warehouse management system (WMS) operations, fleet 
tracking updates, and ERP transaction processing, resulting in an estimated 
revenue impact of INR 3.8 Crore per month.

source_sections.title:
SECTION 1 — Executive Summary and Introduction > 1.2  Current Infrastructure Challenges and Business Drivers > Single-Link 
Dependency
source_sections.full_text:
Single-Link 
Dependency

Hub warehouse sites (Tier-1) currently operate on a single MPLS link with no 
active failover path. Loss of connectivity at a hub site cascades to 8–15 dependent 
spoke sites reliant on the hub for WMS access and IoT aggregation.

paragraphs.text:
Single-Link 
Dependency

Hub warehouse sites (Tier-1) currently operate on a single MPLS link with no 
active failover path. Loss of connectivity at a hub site cascades to 8–15 dependent 
spoke sites reliant on the hub for WMS access and IoT aggregation.

source_sections.title: SECTION 1 — Executive Summary and Introduction > 1.2  Current Infrastructure Challenges and Business Drivers > No Cloud On-Ramp
source_sections.full_text:
No Cloud On-Ramp

Apex is executing a cloud-first transformation programme (ERP on Microsoft 
Azure, data analytics on AWS). Currently, all cloud traffic is backhauled through 
the Mumbai Data Centre, introducing latency of 180–340 ms for branch-to-cloud 
workloads and consuming disproportionate Data Centre bandwidth.

paragraphs.text: No Cloud On-Ramp

Apex is executing a cloud-first transformation programme (ERP on Microsoft 
Azure, data analytics on AWS). Currently, all cloud traffic is backhauled through 
the Mumbai Data Centre, introducing latency of 180–340 ms for branch-to-cloud 
workloads and consuming disproportionate Data Centre bandwidth.

source_sections.title:
SECTION 1 — Executive Summary and Introduction > 1.2  Current Infrastructure Challenges and Business Drivers > Security Perimeter 
Gaps
source_sections.full_text:
Security Perimeter 
Gaps

Branch sites lack next-generation firewall (NGFW) protection. A significant 
ransomware incident in Q3 FY2024 resulted in operational downtime of 72 hours 
across 14 warehouse sites and a forensic investigation cost of INR 1.1 Crore. Apex 
currently has no 24/7 Security Operations Centre (SOC) capability.

paragraphs.text:
Security Perimeter 
Gaps

Branch sites lack next-generation firewall (NGFW) protection. A significant 
ransomware incident in Q3 FY2024 resulted in operational downtime of 72 hours 
across 14 warehouse sites and a forensic investigation cost of INR 1.1 Crore. Apex 
currently has no 24/7 Security Operations Centre (SOC) capability.

source_sections.title:
SECTION 1 — Executive Summary and Introduction > 1.2  Current Infrastructure Challenges and Business Drivers > IoT Connectivity 
Fragmentation
source_sections.full_text:
IoT Connectivity 
Fragmentation

Apex operates a fleet of 2.5 million IoT-enabled tracking SIMs across its vehicle 
fleet and warehouse assets. SIM management is currently distributed across three 
different Mobile Virtual Network Operators (MVNOs), resulting in fragmented 
visibility, inconsistent data plans, and inability to enforce unified MDM policies.

paragraphs.text:
IoT Connectivity 
Fragmentation

Apex operates a fleet of 2.5 million IoT-enabled tracking SIMs across its vehicle 
fleet and warehouse assets. SIM management is currently distributed across three 
different Mobile Virtual Network Operators (MVNOs), resulting in fragmented 
visibility, inconsistent data plans, and inability to enforce unified MDM policies.

source_sections.title:
SECTION 1 — Executive Summary and Introduction > 1.2  Current Infrastructure Challenges and Business Drivers > IoT Connectivity 
Fragmentation > 1.3  Programme Objectives
source_sections.full_text:
1.3  Programme Objectives

Apex Industrial Logistics Limited hereby issues this Request for Proposal to solicit proposals from qualified 
Managed Service Providers and System Integrators for the complete overhaul of its enterprise network, cloud 
connectivity, and security infrastructure. The primary objectives of this programme are:

• 
Decommission all legacy MPLS circuits across 300 branch sites and replace with a fully managed 
SD-WAN overlay with active-active dual-link connectivity at hub sites.
• 
Establish direct, carrier-grade Cloud Interconnects to Microsoft Azure (primary) and Amazon Web 
Services (secondary) from the Mumbai Data Centre, eliminating cloud backhaul latency.

paragraphs.text: 1.3  Programme Objectives

Apex Industrial Logistics Limited hereby issues this Request for Proposal to solicit proposals from qualified 
Managed Service Providers and System Integrators for the complete overhaul of its enterprise network, cloud 
connectivity, and security infrastructure. The primary objectives of this programme are:

• 
Decommission all legacy MPLS circuits across 300 branch sites and replace with a fully managed 
SD-WAN overlay with active-active dual-link connectivity at hub sites.
• 
Establish direct, carrier-grade Cloud Interconnects to Microsoft Azure (primary) and Amazon Web 
Services (secondary) from the Mumbai Data Centre, eliminating cloud backhaul latency.

source_sections.full_text:
2.2  Technology Environment

Apex's current enterprise technology stack is as follows, provided for the purposes of integration planning:

• 
ERP Platform: SAP S/4HANA (hosted on Microsoft Azure — Central India region), migrated in 
FY2023.
• 
Warehouse Management System (WMS): Manhattan Associates WMS — on-premises at Mumbai 
DC, with active Azure DR replication.
• 
Fleet & Asset Tracking: Proprietary IoT platform with 2.5 million active SIMs; data aggregation via 
MQTT brokers hosted on AWS (Mumbai region).
• 
Collaboration: Microsoft 365 (Exchange Online, Teams, SharePoint) — Microsoft Azure hosted.
• 
IT Service Management: ServiceNow (ITSM) — cloud-hosted; ITIL v4 aligned.
• 
Identity: Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure AD) with on-premises Active Directory synchronisation 
via Entra Connect.
• 
Security: Checkpoint firewalls at Mumbai DC (end-of-support 2026); no unified SIEM; no SOC.
• 
Network: Tata Communications MPLS (primary); no SD-WAN; no cloud interconnect.

source_sections.full_text:
2.3  Regulatory and Compliance Context

Apex's network and data infrastructure is subject to the following regulatory frameworks, all of which must be 
addressed in vendor proposals:

• 
Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023 (DPDP Act): All personal data of employees, customers, 
and fleet drivers processed across the network must comply with DPDP Act provisions including 
data localisation, consent management, and data principal rights.
• 
SEBI Cybersecurity and Cyber Resilience Framework (CSCRF) 2024: As a listed entity, Apex is 
subject to SEBI's CSCRF, mandating 24x7 SOC capability, annual VAPT, and Board-level 
cybersecurity governance.
• 
Reserve Bank of India (RBI) Payment Security Guidelines: Applicable to logistics payment and 
settlement flows processed through the network.
• 
ISO/IEC 27001:2022: Apex holds certification and requires all MSP subcontractors to maintain 
equivalent or superior certification.

source_sections.title: SECTION 3 — Project Overview and Objectives > 3.1  Programme Description
source_sections.full_text:
3.1  Programme Description

The Apex Network Transformation Programme (hereinafter "the Programme") is a strategic, enterprise-wide 
initiative to replace the Company's legacy MPLS network infrastructure with a modern, software-defined, cloud-
native managed network and security platform. The Programme encompasses four primary workstreams 
executed in parallel under a unified programme governance structure:

• 
Workstream 1 — Managed SD-WAN Deployment: Full replacement of MPLS circuits across all 300 
branch and hub sites with a managed SD-WAN overlay, incorporating active-active dual-link 
configuration at Tier-1 hub sites.
• 
Workstream 2 — Cloud Direct Interconnect: Establishment of dedicated, carrier-grade private 
interconnects from Apex's Mumbai Data Centre directly to Microsoft Azure (ExpressRoute 
equivalent) and Amazon Web Services (AWS Direct Connect equivalent) at 10 Gbps each.
• 
Workstream 3 — Managed Security Operations Centre: Deployment and continuous 24x7x365 
monitoring of next-generation firewalls at all 300 branch edges by a certified SOC, including threat 
intelligence, SIEM correlation, and SLA-backed incident response.
• 
Workstream 4 — Enterprise IoT Mobility Platform: Consolidation of 2.5 million fleet and asset 
tracking SIMs onto a single enterprise mobility management platform with unified dashboard, eSIM 
capability, and enforced MDM policy.

•

•

source_sections.title: SECTION 3 — Project Overview and Objectives > 3.3  Out of Scope
source_sections.full_text:
3.3  Out of Scope

The following items are explicitly excluded from the scope of this RFP. Vendors must not include pricing for 
out-of-scope items unless explicitly invited to do so in a separate commercial section:

• 
Passive civil or structural works at warehouse sites (cabling beyond network equipment patch 
panels, rack installations beyond those required for SD-WAN CPE).
• 
End-user computing (laptops, desktops, mobile handsets) not related to the IoT fleet tracking 
platform.
• 
SAP S/4HANA or WMS application management or licensing.

source_sections.title: SECTION 4 — Scope of Work and Services > 4.1  Workstream 1: Managed SD-WAN — All 300 Sites > 4.1.1  Site Classification and Link Requirements > Active / Standby > 4.1.2  SD-WAN Platform Mandatory Capabilities
source_sections.full_text:
4.1.2  SD-WAN Platform Mandatory Capabilities

• 
Policy-based intelligent traffic steering with Application-Aware Routing for classified applications 
including SAP S/4HANA, Microsoft Teams, Manhattan WMS, and IoT MQTT traffic.
• 
Dynamic Path Selection with real-time link quality measurement (latency, jitter, packet loss) with sub-
50 ms failover between primary and secondary links.
• 
Zero-Touch Provisioning (ZTP): All CPE devices must be provisioned remotely without on-site 
engineering resources. ZTP must enable a branch go-live within 4 hours of hardware delivery on-
site.
• 
Centralised management via a vendor-provided cloud-managed controller with full Apex read-only 
portal access, displaying per-site topology, link health, and traffic analytics.
• 
BGP and OSPF routing support with automated failover; static routing where dynamic protocols are 
not feasible at Tier-3 sites.
• 
Quality of Service (QoS) with a minimum of 8 traffic classes, including guaranteed bandwidth 
allocation for WMS and SAP traffic at all Tier-1 and Tier-2 sites.
• 
Integrated next-generation firewall (NGFW) capability at the SD-WAN edge CPE, including URL 
filtering, IPS/IDS, and SSL inspection, with policy managed from the SOC (Workstream 3).
• 
Support for 1:N VPN hub-and-spoke and any-to-any mesh topologies to enable direct branch-to-
branch connectivity for IoT data aggregation.
• 
Vendor must explicitly state the OEM hardware partner(s) proposed for SD-WAN CPE (e.g., Cisco 
Catalyst SD-WAN / Meraki, Fortinet FortiGate SD-WAN, VMware VeloCloud, Palo Alto Prisma SD-
WAN). Proposals specifying 'TBD' for hardware OEM will be deemed Non-Compliant.

4.2  Workstream 2: Cloud Direct Interconnects

Apex requires the establishment of two (2) dedicated, private, carrier-grade Cloud Interconnects from its 
primary Mumbai Data Centre (NESCO IT Park, Goregaon East) as follows:

• 
Microsoft Azure ExpressRoute: Two (2) x 10 Gbps dedicated ExpressRoute circuits to the Microsoft 
Azure Central India region (pune1 / pune2 peering locations or equivalent Mumbai-region peering). 
Circuits must be configured in an active-active ECMP load-sharing arrangement with full BGP route 
redundancy. The interconnect must support both Azure Private Peering (for SAP and WMS 
workloads) and Microsoft Peering (for Microsoft 365).
• 
Amazon Web Services Direct Connect: One (1) x 10 Gbps Dedicated Connection to the AWS 
Mumbai (ap-south-1) region via a Direct Connect Location in Mumbai (STT GDC Hua Wei Navi

4.3  Workstream 3: 24/7 Managed Security Operations Centre (SOC)

Apex requires a fully outsourced, 24x7x365 Managed SOC service to monitor, detect, investigate, and respond 
to cybersecurity threats across its entire network estate.

paragraphs.text: 4.3  Workstream 3: 24/7 Managed Security Operations Centre (SOC)

Apex requires a fully outsourced, 24x7x365 Managed SOC service to monitor, detect, investigate, and respond 
to cybersecurity threats across its entire network estate.

source_sections.title:
SECTION 4 — Scope of Work and Services > 4.1  Workstream 1: Managed SD-WAN — All 300 Sites > 4.1.1  Site Classification and Link Requirements > Active / Standby > 4.2  Workstream 2: Cloud Direct Interconnects > APEX INDUSTRIAL LOGISTICS LIMITED
RFP Ref: AILL-PROC-2025-0078  |  STRICTLY > 4.3  Workstream 3: 24/7 Managed Security Operations Centre (SOC) > 4.3.1  SOC Service Scope
source_sections.full_text:
4.3.1  SOC Service Scope

• 
Continuous monitoring of all 300 SD-WAN edge NGFW devices for security events, policy violations, 
and anomalous traffic patterns.
• 
Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) — Vendor must operate a cloud-delivered 
SIEM platform (e.g., Microsoft Sentinel, Splunk, IBM QRadar, or equivalent) ingesting logs from all 
edge firewalls, the Mumbai DC, cloud interconnects, and endpoint detection agents.
• 
Threat Intelligence integration: Vendor must subscribe to at least two (2) commercial Threat 
Intelligence feeds (e.g., Recorded Future, CrowdStrike Intelligence, Mandiant) and correlate IOCs 
against Apex's network traffic in near-real-time.
• 
Vulnerability Management: Monthly automated VAPT scans across all in-scope network devices and 
quarterly manual penetration testing of the network perimeter, with findings reported to Apex CISO 
within 5 business days.
• 
Incident Response: The vendor must provide Tier-1, Tier-2, and Tier-3 analyst capability within the 
SOC, with defined escalation paths. P1 incidents must be acknowledged within 15 minutes and 
contained within 4 hours. P1 is defined as any confirmed active threat affecting more than 5 sites or 
the Mumbai DC.
• 
Root Cause Analysis (RCA): For all P1 and P2 security incidents, a formal RCA report must be 
delivered to Apex within 72 hours of incident containment, conforming to ITIL v4 Problem 
Management process.
• 
Monthly SOC Operations Report: Delivered by the 5th business day of the following month, 
containing: event volumes, MTTD, MTTR, top threats, false-positive rate, and open vulnerability 
status.

source_sections.full_text:
4.3.2  Data Residency for SOC

• 
All SIEM data, log archives, and security event records relating to Apex network traffic must be 
stored exclusively within India-based data centres (Tier III or above). Storage outside India is 
prohibited without explicit written authorisation from Apex CISO, consistent with DPDP Act 2023 
requirements.
• 
The vendor must provide the name, location, and third-party audit certification of the data centre(s) 
used for SIEM log storage as part of the proposal.

source_sections.full_text:
4.4  Workstream 4: Enterprise IoT and Mobility Connectivity

Apex operates 2.5 million IoT SIMs across vehicle fleet trackers, warehouse asset tags, and cold-chain 
temperature sensors. The vendor must provide:

• 
A unified Enterprise Mobility Management (EMM) platform with a single dashboard enabling real-
time visibility of all 2.5 million SIMs, including active/inactive status, data consumption, location 
(where applicable), and MDM policy compliance.
• 
National Data Connectivity: Coverage across all 28 operational states with indoor and outdoor data 
coverage. The vendor must provide a coverage heatmap for all Tier-1 and Tier-2 site locations as 
part of the proposal.
• 
eSIM / eUICC Support: The platform must support eSIM remote provisioning for new device 
additions and SIM profile switching without physical SIM replacement.

reporting.

traverse the public internet and is routed directly to Apex's network via private peering or MPLS 
handoff to the SD-WAN fabric.
• 
Data Plan Flexibility: Vendors must offer pooled data plans across SIM segments (fleet, assets, cold-
chain) to enable efficient data allocation and prevent per-SIM overage charges.
• 
API Integration: The EMM platform must expose RESTful APIs for integration with Apex's proprietary 
IoT fleet management platform for automated SIM activation, deactivation, and data consumption 
reporting.

APEX INDUSTRIAL LOGISTICS LIMITED

source_sections.title: SECTION 5 — Technical and Functional Requirements > 5.1  Instructions for Completion
source_sections.full_text:
5.1  Instructions for Completion

This section contains the definitive technical and functional requirements for the Network Transformation 
Programme. Vendors MUST respond to EVERY requirement listed in the matrix below. The following response 
codes apply:

• 
Compliant (C): The vendor's proposed solution fully satisfies the stated requirement in its standard, 
generally available form without bespoke development, workarounds, or future roadmap 
commitments.
• 
Partially Compliant (PC): The proposed solution partially satisfies the requirement. The 'Vendor 
Remarks' column MUST contain a specific description of the gap, the proposed resolution, and the 
estimated timeline to achieve full compliance.
• 
Non-Compliant (NC): The proposed solution does not satisfy the requirement. The 'Vendor Remarks' 
column MUST contain a full explanation. Proposals with more than four (4) Non-Compliant 
responses to Mandatory requirements will be disqualified.

VENDOR INSTRUCTION: Do NOT alter, re-number, or re-sequence Requirement IDs. Do NOT 
modify the column structure of the requirements table. Responses must be entered exclusively in 
the 'Vendor Response' and 'Vendor Remarks' columns. Any modification to the table structure will 
render the proposal non-evaluable.

SOC — Availability

The Managed SOC service MUST 
operate 24 hours per day, 7 days 
per week, 365 days per year 
(including Indian public holidays) 
with no scheduled maintenance 
windows that reduce monitoring 
coverage below Tier-1 analyst 
staffing levels.

SOC — SIEM 
Platform

The SIEM platform used by the 
SOC MUST ingest logs from all 
300 SD-WAN edge devices, the 
Mumbai DC, and cloud 
interconnects. The vendor must 
name the SIEM product proposed 
(e.g., Microsoft Sentinel, Splunk 
Enterprise Security, IBM QRadar, 
or equivalent). SIEM data MUST 
be stored in India.

NC]

Response SLAs

acknowledged within 15 minutes 
and contained within 4 hours. P2 
incidents MUST be acknowledged 
within 30 minutes and resolved 
within 8 hours. P3 incidents within 
2 hours acknowledgement and 24 
hours resolution. All timings 
measured 24x7.

Mandatory

[C / PC / 
NC]

[Vendor to 
fill]

Management, and Change 
Management processes

containment. Vendor must provide 
sample RCA template with 
proposal.

NC]

fill]

million SIMs at Apex's option. 
Vendor must provide the GSMA 
eSIM RSP (Remote SIM 
Provisioning) compliance 
certificate with the proposal.

Mandatory

[C / PC / 
NC]

[Vendor to 
fill]

TR-021
IoT — National 
Coverage

The vendor MUST provide 
demonstrable 4G LTE network 
coverage (own network or 
roaming agreement) covering a 
minimum of 98% of Apex's Tier-1 
and Tier-2 site locations across all 
28 operational states. A geo-
referenced coverage report must 
be submitted with the proposal.

Mandatory

[C / PC / 
NC]

[Vendor to 
fill]

TR-022
IoT — API 
Integration

The EMM platform MUST expose 
a documented RESTful API 
(OpenAPI 3.0 specification) for 
SIM lifecycle management 
(activation, deactivation, 
suspension, data plan change) 
and consumption reporting. API 
documentation must be provided 
as Appendix F.

Mandatory

[C / PC / 
NC]

[Vendor to 
fill]

TR-023
General — 
Disaster Recovery

The vendor's managed service 
platform (SD-WAN controller, 
SOC SIEM, EMM platform) must 
operate from geo-redundant 
infrastructure within India with an 
RPO of less than 15 minutes and 
an RTO of less than 60 minutes 
for all Tier-1 managed services.

Mandatory

[C / PC / 
NC]

[Vendor to 
fill]

TR-024

General — 
ServiceNow 
Integration

The vendor's NOC and SOC 
ticketing systems MUST integrate 
bi-directionally with Apex's 
ServiceNow ITSM instance for 
automated incident creation, 
status updates, and closure. 
Vendor must describe the 
integration mechanism (native 
connector / REST API / email) 
and provide a sample integration 
architecture.

Mandatory

[C / PC / 
NC]

[Vendor to 
fill]

TR-025
General — 
Reporting

Vendor MUST provide a unified 
executive dashboard and monthly 
managed service report covering 
network availability, cloud 
interconnect performance, SOC 
incident statistics, IoT SIM health, 
and SLA performance against all 
KPIs defined in Section 3.2.

Proposals will be evaluated against the following programme-level success criteria, which will be incorporated 
as contractual Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) in the resulting Master Services Agreement:

KPI Reference
Success Criterion
Target / Threshold

Network Availability — Tier-1 Hub Sites
≥ 99.99% monthly

KPI-02
Network Availability — Tier-2 Branch Sites
≥ 99.95% monthly

KPI-03
SD-WAN Failover Time (primary to secondary link)
< 50 milliseconds

KPI-04
Cloud Interconnect Latency (Mumbai DC to Azure 
Central India)
< 8 ms round-trip

KPI-05
SOC — P1 Security Incident Acknowledgement
< 15 minutes

KPI-06
SOC — P1 Security Incident Containment
< 4 hours

KPI-07
Migration Completion — All 300 Sites
Within 18 months of 
contract execution

KPI-08
Zero-Touch Provisioning — New Branch Site
< 4 hours from hardware 
delivery

KPI-09
MTTR — Network Incident (Branch Site)
< 4 hours

KPI-10
IoT SIM Activation (eSIM / ICCID)
< 2 hours from portal 
request

Mandatory

[C / PC / 
NC]

[Vendor to 
fill]

source_sections.title: SECTION 7 — Proposal Submission Instructions > 7.1  Submission Channel
source_sections.full_text:
7.1  Submission Channel

All 
proposals 
must 
be 
submitted 
exclusively 
via 
the 
Apex 
official 
procurement 
portal 
at: 
https://procurement.apexlogistics.in/rfp/AILL-PROC-2025-0078

• 
Proposals submitted by email, courier, hand delivery, or any channel other than the procurement 
portal will be rejected without evaluation, regardless of the reason for non-portal submission.
• 
Proposals must be received by 17:00 IST on 21 November 2025. The portal will automatically close 
at this time. No late submissions will be accepted under any circumstances.
• 
Proposals must be uploaded as a single PDF/A (ISO 19005 compliant) package. Editable Word 
documents, spreadsheets, or ZIP archives as the primary submission are not accepted.
• 
Vendors will receive an automated acknowledgement of receipt within 30 minutes of successful 
submission. If no acknowledgement is received within this period, vendors must raise a portal 
access query via rfp-queries@apexlogistics.in immediately.

source_sections.full_text:
Requirement
Specification

Format
Single consolidated PDF/A (ISO 19005 compliant) containing the complete 
proposal, including all appendices and supporting documents.

Language
English only. All technical specifications, SLA commitments, and commercial 
terms must be in English.

Page Limit
Main body (Sections 1–12): Maximum 120 pages (A4, minimum 11pt Arial, 
25mm margins). Appendices are not subject to a page limit.

Section Order
Proposals must follow the identical section numbering as this RFP. Reordering 
of sections is not permitted.

7.4  Clarifications and Q&A Process

• 
All queries relating to this RFP must be submitted via the procurement portal Q&A module by 31 
October 2025. Queries submitted after this deadline will not be answered.
• 
Queries must be submitted in English. Queries in other languages will not be answered.
• 
Aether Networks' compiled responses will be published simultaneously to all registered vendors on 
10 November 2025. Responses published via the Q&A process form part of this RFP as binding 
amendments.
• 
Direct communication with any Apex employee outside the authorised procurement portal is strictly 
prohibited and will result in immediate disqualification.

source_sections.title: SECTION 9 — Evaluation Criteria and Scoring Methodology
source_sections.full_text: SECTION 9 — Evaluation Criteria and Scoring Methodology

paragraphs.text: SECTION 9 — Evaluation Criteria and Scoring Methodology

source_sections.title: SECTION 9 — Evaluation Criteria and Scoring Methodology > 9.1  Evaluation Methodology
source_sections.full_text:
9.1  Evaluation Methodology

Proposals will be evaluated by the Apex Technical Evaluation Committee (TEC) using a two-stage weighted 
scoring methodology:

• 
Stage 1 — Administrative and Pre-Qualification Review: Verification of mandatory pre-qualification 
criteria (Section 8.1). Pass/Fail gate. Proposals failing Stage 1 are eliminated without scoring.
• 
Stage 2 — Technical, Commercial, Security, and Reference Scoring: Scored evaluation across four 
dimensions as defined in Section 9.2.

Vendors shortlisted following Stage 2 (top 3 by score) will be invited to deliver a live technical demonstration 
and proof of concept (PoC) at Apex HQ, Bhiwandi. The PoC scenario brief will be issued no later than 5 
business days prior. PoC outcome will be weighted at 20% of the final Stage 2 score.

[Referenced Section 9.2]: [Section: SECTION 9 — Evaluation Criteria and Scoring Methodology > 9.2  Weighted Scoring Model]

Evaluation Dimension

Sub-Criteria

20%
•

— 8%
•

• 
Migration plan quality (phasing, ZTP, risk management) 
— 6%

•

term — 18%
•

•

architecture — 4%

•

• 
Verified client reference calls (shortlisted vendors only) — 
4%

TOTAL
100%
Minimum qualifying score to proceed to BAFO: 65 out of 100

source_sections.full_text: SECTION 10 — Pricing and Cost Proposal Requirements

paragraphs.text: SECTION 10 — Pricing and Cost Proposal Requirements

source_sections.title: SECTION 10 — Pricing and Cost Proposal Requirements > 10.1  Pricing Principles
source_sections.full_text:
10.1  Pricing Principles

The following mandatory commercial conditions apply to all pricing submitted in response to this RFP. 
Proposals not conforming to these conditions will be deemed commercially non-compliant:

• 
Price Validity: All unit prices and commercial rates must remain firm, fixed, and binding for a 
minimum period of 180 calendar days from the Final Proposal Submission Deadline.
• 
Currency: All pricing must be denominated in Indian Rupees (INR). Pricing denominated in foreign 
currencies without an INR equivalent will not be accepted.
• 
Tax Exclusivity: All prices must be stated exclusive of GST. GST will be assessed separately at the 
applicable rate.
• 
Itemised Pricing: Bundled lump-sum prices are not acceptable. Each line item must be individually 
priced to the granularity specified in Section 10.2.
• 
OpEx Hardware Model: All SD-WAN CPE hardware, edge firewall hardware, and any other network 
equipment required for this programme must be provided under a Vendor-owned, OpEx (Hardware-
as-a-Service) model. Apex will not purchase or capitalise network hardware. Vendors proposing 
CapEx hardware models will be scored negatively on commercial evaluation.
• 
Most Favoured Nation: Apex requires a Most Favoured Nation (MFN) clause in the resulting 
contract, guaranteeing that pricing offered to Apex is no less favourable than pricing offered to any 
comparable Indian enterprise customer of the vendor.

source_sections.title:
SECTION 10 — Pricing and Cost Proposal Requirements > 10.2  Pricing Response Table > APEX INDUSTRIAL LOGISTICS LIMITED
RFP Ref: AILL-PROC-2025-0078  |  STRICTLY > 10.3  Commercial Narrative
source_sections.full_text:
10.3  Commercial Narrative

In addition to the Pricing Response Table, vendors must provide a written commercial narrative (maximum 6 
pages) addressing:

• 
Total Cost of Ownership (TCO) over the 36-month contract term, segmented by Year 1 
(implementation and stabilisation) and Years 2–3 (steady-state operations). TCO must include all 
NRC and MRC components.
• 
Payment milestone schedule: Vendors must propose payment milestones linked to programme 
delivery milestones (e.g., Phase 1 completion, Phase 3 completion, Phase 5 acceptance).
• 
Volume discount tiers for IoT SIM counts (at 2.5M, 3M, and 4M SIMs).
• 
Commercial treatment for out-of-scope site additions (new warehouse openings) and for early 
termination of legacy MPLS circuits with carrier assistance.
• 
Hardware refresh commitment: Vendor must commit to a hardware refresh cycle for all SD-WAN 
CPE not exceeding 5 years from initial deployment, at no additional cost to Apex.

source_sections.title: SECTION 11 — Terms and Conditions / Contractual Requirements
source_sections.full_text:
The following contractual terms will govern the Master Services Agreement (MSA) resulting from this 
procurement. Vendors must explicitly acknowledge each clause in their proposal. Proposed deviations must 
be submitted in the Deviations Register (Appendix G) with full commercial and legal justification.

paragraphs.text:
The following contractual terms will govern the Master Services Agreement (MSA) resulting from this 
procurement. Vendors must explicitly acknowledge each clause in their proposal. Proposed deviations must 
be submitted in the Deviations Register (Appendix G) with full commercial and legal justification.

source_sections.title: SECTION 11 — Terms and Conditions / Contractual Requirements > 11.1  Contract Term
source_sections.full_text:
11.1  Contract Term

The initial contract term is thirty-six (36) months from the date of contract execution, with two (2) optional 
renewal periods of twelve (12) months each, exercisable at Apex's sole discretion with sixty (60) days' written 
notice prior to the end of each contract term.

DELIBERATE TRAP CLAUSE — LEGAL REVIEW REQUIRED: The 36-month minimum contract 
term is included deliberately to test vendor legal pushback. Vendors are expected to negotiate a 
break clause at month 18 and a 24-month initial term option. Vendors accepting 36 months 
without deviation may be disadvantaged in final evaluation, as inflexibility on contract term is 
considered a commercial risk by Apex. Vendors must address this in Appendix G.

[Referenced Appendix G]: [Section: SECTION 7 — Proposal Submission Instructions > 7.2  Proposal Structure and Format Requirements > Deviations]

Deviations

Any deviations from this RFP's requirements or proposed contractual terms 
must be compiled in a single 'Deviations Register' table as Appendix G. 
Deviations embedded in the main proposal text without corresponding 
Appendix G entry will be disregarded.

source_sections.title: SECTION 11 — Terms and Conditions / Contractual Requirements > 11.2  Payment Terms
source_sections.full_text:
11.2  Payment Terms

Payment terms for all monthly recurring charges (MRC) and one-time non-recurring charges (NRC) shall be 
as follows:

• 
Monthly Recurring Charges: Payable within ninety (90) days of invoice date (Net-90 days from 
invoice). Invoices must be submitted on the first business day of each calendar month for the 
preceding month's services.
• 
One-Time Non-Recurring Charges: Payable within ninety (90) days of invoice date, subject to Apex's 
acceptance of the relevant programme milestone.

DELIBERATE TRAP CLAUSE — CASH FLOW RISK: Net-90 payment terms are commercially 
aggressive and are included to test vendor risk assessment. Industry standard for managed 
services is Net-30 to Net-45. Vendors who accept Net-90 without a late payment interest clause 
risk significant cash flow exposure. Vendors must propose a deviation in Appendix G, specifying 
proposed standard payment terms and a late payment interest rate consistent with the MSME 
Development Act, 2006 (where applicable) or contractual interest at the RBI repo rate plus 2%.

[Referenced Appendix G]: [Section: SECTION 7 — Proposal Submission Instructions > 7.2  Proposal Structure and Format Requirements > Deviations]

Deviations

Any deviations from this RFP's requirements or proposed contractual terms 
must be compiled in a single 'Deviations Register' table as Appendix G. 
Deviations embedded in the main proposal text without corresponding 
Appendix G entry will be disregarded.

source_sections.title: SECTION 11 — Terms and Conditions / Contractual Requirements > 11.3  Liability
source_sections.full_text:
11.3  Liability

• 
General Liability Cap: The vendor's total aggregate liability under the MSA for any and all claims in 
any 12-month period shall not exceed the total charges paid by Apex to the vendor in the preceding 
twelve (12) months.
• 
Data Breach and Catastrophic Failure: Notwithstanding the general liability cap, in the event of: (a) a 
confirmed data breach attributable to the vendor's negligence or wilful misconduct resulting in the 
exfiltration of personal data of more than 10,000 Apex data subjects; or (b) a complete network 
outage affecting more than 50 Tier-2 or Tier-3 sites simultaneously for a period exceeding 8 
continuous hours; the vendor shall be subject to unlimited liability with no financial cap.
• 
Consequential Loss: Subject to the unlimited liability carve-out above, neither party shall be liable to 
the other for any indirect, special, or consequential losses.

DELIBERATE TRAP CLAUSE — UNLIMITED LIABILITY: The 'unlimited liability' provision for data 
breaches and catastrophic outages is included deliberately to test vendor legal diligence. No 
commercially reasonable vendor should accept unlimited liability. Vendors must propose in 
Appendix G a negotiated super-cap (e.g., 3x annual contract value) with specific carve-outs for 
gross negligence and wilful misconduct, and cyber liability insurance coverage minimums 
consistent with SEBI CSCRF requirements.

[Referenced Appendix G]: [Section: SECTION 7 — Proposal Submission Instructions > 7.2  Proposal Structure and Format Requirements > Deviations]

Deviations

Any deviations from this RFP's requirements or proposed contractual terms 
must be compiled in a single 'Deviations Register' table as Appendix G. 
Deviations embedded in the main proposal text without corresponding 
Appendix G entry will be disregarded.

source_sections.title:
SECTION 11 — Terms and Conditions / Contractual Requirements > 11.3  Liability > APEX INDUSTRIAL LOGISTICS LIMITED
RFP Ref: AILL-PROC-2025-0078  |  STRICTLY > 11.4  SLA Penalties and Service Credits > P3
Non-Critical Incident
source_sections.full_text:
P3
Non-Critical Incident

Single site outage exceeding 4-hour 
MTTR, or SLA reporting delay 
exceeding 30 minutes from incident 
detection.

0.5% of total monthly invoice 
value per incident.

• 
SLA credit accumulation is capped at 25% of the total monthly invoice value per calendar month. 
Accumulated credits exceeding the cap carry over to the subsequent month.
• 
Repeated P1 incidents (three or more within any rolling 90-day period) constitute a material breach, 
entitling Apex to terminate the MSA for cause with thirty (30) days' written notice without payment of 
any termination penalty.

DELIBERATE TRAP CLAUSE — PENALTY APPLIED AGAINST TOTAL CONTRACT VALUE: The P1 
penalty provision states credits are applied against the 'Total Contract Value'. This is 
commercially punitive and atypical. Market standard for managed services is to cap penalties 
against the monthly invoice value of the affected site(s), not the Total Contract Value. Vendors 
must flag this in Appendix G and propose a commercially reasonable alternative: e.g., penalties 
applied against the monthly MRC of the directly affected sites only, with a reasonable monthly 
cap.

[Referenced Appendix G]: [Section: SECTION 7 — Proposal Submission Instructions > 7.2  Proposal Structure and Format Requirements > Deviations]

Deviations

Any deviations from this RFP's requirements or proposed contractual terms 
must be compiled in a single 'Deviations Register' table as Appendix G. 
Deviations embedded in the main proposal text without corresponding 
Appendix G entry will be disregarded.

source_sections.full_text:
11.5  Intellectual Property

All deliverables, documentation, custom configuration scripts, runbooks, and management tools created 
specifically for the Apex programme under this MSA shall be the intellectual property of Apex upon full 
payment. Pre-existing vendor IP incorporated into deliverables shall remain the vendor's IP, subject to a 
perpetual, royalty-free licence to Apex to use such IP for the purposes of operating the managed services.

source_sections.full_text:
11.6  Governing Law and Dispute Resolution

The MSA and all related agreements shall be governed by the laws of India. Any dispute shall first be subject 
to executive escalation (30 days), followed by mediation under the ICADR (Indian Council of Arbitration and 
Dispute Resolution) rules (60 days), and finally arbitration under the Arbitration and Conciliation Act, 1996, 
seated in Mumbai, Maharashtra.

APEX INDUSTRIAL LOGISTICS LIMITED
RFP Ref: AILL-PROC-2025-0078  |  STRICTLY
source_sections.full_text:
CONFIDENTIAL

Managed Network Services & Cybersecurity RFP
Page  
APEX INDUSTRIAL LOGISTICS LIMITED

— END OF REQUEST FOR PROPOSAL DOCUMENT —

RFP Reference: AILL-PROC-2025-0078  |  Issuer: Apex Industrial Logistics Limited  |  Issue Date: 10 October 2025

© 2025 Apex Industrial Logistics Limited. All Rights Reserved. Unauthorised reproduction or distribution is prohibited.

Pain Point | Current Impact on Operations
Legacy MPLS Network | All 300 branch warehouses are connected via legacy Multiprotocol Label Switching (MPLS) circuits procured from a single incumbent carrier. Bandwidth tiers are rigid (2 Mbps to 10 Mbps per branch), costly to upgrade, and require 60–90-day lead times for any configuration change.
Chronic Network Outages | The Company experienced an average of 4.3 unplanned network outages per month per region in FY2024–25, each lasting an average of 47 minutes. These outages directly halt warehouse management system (WMS) operations, fleet tracking updates, and ERP transaction processing, resulting in an estimated revenue impact of INR 3.8 Crore per month.
Single-Link Dependency | Hub warehouse sites (Tier-1) currently operate on a single MPLS link with no active failover path. Loss of connectivity at a hub site cascades to 8–15 dependent spoke sites reliant on the hub for WMS access and IoT aggregation.
No Cloud On-Ramp | Apex is executing a cloud-first transformation programme (ERP on Microsoft Azure, data analytics on AWS). Currently, all cloud traffic is backhauled through the Mumbai Data Centre, introducing latency of 180–340 ms for branch-to-cloud workloads and consuming disproportionate Data Centre bandwidth.
Security Perimeter Gaps | Branch sites lack next-generation firewall (NGFW) protection. A significant ransomware incident in Q3 FY2024 resulted in operational downtime of 72 hours across 14 warehouse sites and a forensic investigation cost of INR 1.1 Crore. Apex currently has no 24/7 Security Operations Centre (SOC) capability.
IoT Connectivity Fragmentation | Apex operates a fleet of 2.5 million IoT-enabled tracking SIMs across its vehicle fleet and warehouse assets. SIM Management is currently distributed across three different Mobile Virtual Network Operators (MVNOs), resulting in fragmented visibility, inconsistent data plans, and inability to enforce unified MDM policies.

Attribute | Detail
Registered Name | Apex Industrial Logistics Limited
CIN | L74899MH1994PLC076432
Headquarters | Plot 14, Logistics Park, Bhiwandi, Thane, Maharashtra — 421 302, India
Data Centre | Primary Tier-III DC — NESCO IT Park, Goregaon East, Mumbai, Maharashtra
Annual Revenue (FY25) | Approx. INR 5,500 Crore
Employees | Approx. 15,000 (direct); 28,000 (including contract workforce)
Operational Sites | 300 branch warehouses; 12 regional hub facilities; 1 primary Data Centre; 2 Disaster Recovery sites
Geographic Presence | 28 Indian States and Union Territories; Cross-border operations in Nepal, Bangladesh, and Sri Lanka
Stock Exchange Listing | National Stock Exchange (NSE: APEXIL); BSE Limited (BSE: 539876)
IT Governance | COBIT 2019 Framework; ISO/IEC 27001:2022 certified (STQC, Cert-In accredited)

KPI Reference | Success Criterion | Target / Threshold
KPI-01 | Network Availability — Tier-1 Hub Sites | ≥ 99.99% monthly
KPI-02 | Network Availability — Tier-2 Branch Sites | ≥ 99.95% monthly
KPI-03 | SD-WAN Failover Time (primary to secondary link) | < 50 milliseconds
KPI-04 | Cloud Interconnect Latency (Mumbai DC to Azure Central India) | < 8 ms round-trip
KPI-05 | SOC — P1 Security Incident Acknowledgement | < 15 minutes
KPI-06 | SOC — P1 Security Incident Containment | < 4 hours
KPI-07 | Migration Completion — All 300 Sites | Within 18 months of contract execution
KPI-08 | Zero-Touch Provisioning — New Branch Site | < 4 hours from hardware delivery
KPI-09 | MTTR — Network Incident (Branch Site) | < 4 hours
KPI-10 | IoT SIM Activation (eSIM / ICCID) | < 2 hours from portal request

Site Tier | Site Count | Primary Link | Secondary Link | Link Mode
Tier-1 Hub | 12 sites | Broadband / Dedicated Fibre — 100 Mbps min | 4G/5G LTE — 50 Mbps min | Active / Active
Tier-2 Branch | 228 sites | Broadband Fibre — 50 Mbps min | 4G LTE Backup — 20 Mbps min | Active / Standby
Tier-3 Remote | 60 sites | 4G/5G LTE — 20 Mbps min | VSAT (where terrestrial unavailable) | Active / Standby

Req. ID | Category | Description | Priority | Vendor Response | Vendor Remarks
TR-001 | SD-WAN — Architecture | The SD-WAN solution MUST be a cloud-managed, fully software-defined overlay capable of supporting all 300 Apex branch sites within a single management domain without requiring a hardware controller at the Data Centre. | Mandatory | [C / PC / NC] | [Vendor to fill]
TR-002 | SD-WAN — ZTP | Zero-Touch Provisioning (ZTP) MUST be supported for all CPE devices. A branch site MUST be fully operational within 4 hours of CPE delivery, without requiring an on-site engineer from the vendor. | Mandatory | [C / PC / NC] | [Vendor to fill]
TR-003 | SD-WAN — Failover | Dynamic path failover from primary to secondary link MUST complete in under 50 milliseconds as measured end-to-end from packet loss detection to traffic rerouting, without manual intervention. | Mandatory | [C / PC / NC] | [Vendor to fill]
TR-004 | SD-WAN — BGP | The solution MUST support BGP routing with automated route advertisement and withdrawal upon link failure, supporting integration with Apex's Mumbai DC edge router (Cisco ASR 1002-HX) via eBGP peering. | Mandatory | [C / PC / NC] | [Vendor to fill]

TR-ID | Requirement | Description | Compliance | Vendor Response
TR-005 | SD-WAN — OEM Hardware | Vendor MUST specify the OEM hardware partner(s) for SD-WAN CPE (e.g., Cisco, Fortinet, VMware Velocloud, Palo Alto). CPE must be provided as-a-Service under OpEx pricing. Vendor-owned hardware is mandatory; Apex will not purchase CPE outright. | Mandatory | [C / PC / NC] [Vendor to fill — Name OEM]
TR-006 | SD-WAN — QoS | The solution MUST support a minimum of 8 QoS traffic classes with guaranteed bandwidth (GBW) and maximum bandwidth (MBW) policies configurable per application, per site. Guaranteed bandwidth for SAP S/4HANA and WMS traffic must be configurable independently of other traffic classes. | Mandatory | [C / PC / NC] [Vendor to fill]
TR-007 | SD-WAN — Security | Integrated NGFW capability at the SD-WAN edge MUST include IPS/IDS, URL filtering (minimum 60 URL categories), DNS security, and SSL deep inspection. Vendor must specify the OEM firewall solution (e.g., Fortinet FortiGate, Palo Alto Networks NGFW, Check Point). | Mandatory | [C / PC / NC] [Vendor to fill — Name Firewall OEM]
TR-008 | SD-WAN — Portal | A self-service portal with role-based access control (RBAC) MUST be provided to Apex IT staff with read-only visibility of network topology, link health, traffic statistics, and active alerts across all 300 sites. No additional licensing cost for portal access. | Mandatory | [C / PC / NC] [Vendor to fill]
TR-009 | SD-WAN — Project Mgmt | Vendor MUST provide a detailed 300-site migration project plan conforming to either PRINCE2 or PMBOK methodology. A dedicated Project Manager (minimum PMP or PRINCE2 Practitioner certified) must be named in the proposal and committed to the programme through completion. | Mandatory | [C / PC / NC] [Vendor to fill]
TR-010 | Cloud Interconnect — Azure | Vendor MUST hold an active Microsoft Azure ExpressRoute Partner designation and provide two (2) x 10 Gbps circuits in active-active configuration to the Azure Central India region. Evidence of Microsoft partner designation MUST be attached as Appendix B. | Mandatory | [C / PC / NC] [Vendor to fill]
TR-011 | Cloud Interconnect — AWS | Vendor MUST hold an active AWS Direct Connect Delivery Partner designation and provide one (1) x 10 Gbps Dedicated Connection to the AWS ap-south-1 Mumbai region. Evidence of AWS partner designation MUST be attached as Appendix B. | Mandatory | [C / PC / NC] [Vendor to fill]

TR-ID | Requirement | Description | Compliance Status | Vendor Response
TR-012 | Cloud Interconnect — Latency | The Azure ExpressRoute interconnect MUST achieve a maximum round-trip latency of 8 ms between the Apex Mumbai DC and the Azure Central India region under steady-state traffic conditions. Vendor must provide benchmark test evidence from equivalent deployments. | Mandatory | [C / PC / NC] [Vendor to fill]
TR-013 | SOC — Availability | The Managed SOC service MUST operate 24 hours per day, 7 days per week, 365 days per year (including Indian public holidays) with no scheduled maintenance windows that reduce monitoring coverage below Tier-1 analyst staffing levels. | Mandatory | [C / PC / NC] [Vendor to fill]
TR-014 | SOC — SIEM Platform | The SIEM platform used by the SOC MUST ingest logs from all 300 SD-WAN edge devices, the Mumbai DC, and cloud interconnects. The vendor must name the SIEM product proposed (e.g., Microsoft Sentinel, Splunk Enterprise Security, IBM QRadar, or equivalent). SIEM data MUST be stored in India. | Mandatory | [C / PC / NC] [Vendor to fill — Name SIEM]
TR-015 | SOC — Incident Response SLAs | P1 incidents MUST be acknowledged within 15 minutes and contained within 4 hours. P2 incidents MUST be acknowledged within 30 minutes and resolved within 8 hours. P3 incidents within 2 hours acknowledgement and 24 hours resolution. All timings measured 24x7. | Mandatory | [C / PC / NC] [Vendor to fill]
TR-016 | SOC — ITIL Alignment | The SOC MUST operate Incident Management, Problem Management, and Change Management processes conforming to ITIL v4. Root Cause Analysis (RCA) reports for P1 and P2 incidents MUST be delivered within 72 hours of incident containment. Vendor must provide sample RCA template with proposal. | Mandatory | [C / PC / NC] [Vendor to fill]
TR-017 | SOC — Threat Intelligence | Vendor MUST demonstrate active subscriptions to a minimum of two (2) commercial Threat Intelligence platforms and provide evidence of integration with the proposed SIEM, including IOC ingestion frequency and automated blocking workflow. | Mandatory | [C / PC / NC] [Vendor to fill]
TR-018 | Compliance — DPDP Act 2023 | Vendor MUST provide a written Data Privacy Impact Assessment (DPIA) framework for the proposed solution, demonstrating how personal data of Apex employees and customers processed across the network is handled in compliance with the Digital Personal Data Protection | Mandatory | [C / PC / NC] [Vendor to fill]

Requirement ID | Requirement Description | Compliance Status | Vendor Response
TR-019 | IoT — Private APN | Mandatory | [C / PC / NC]
TR-020 | IoT — eSIM | Mandatory | [C / PC / NC]
TR-021 | IoT — National Coverage | Mandatory | [C / PC / NC]
TR-022 | IoT — API Integration | Mandatory | [C / PC / NC]
TR-023 | General — Disaster Recovery | Mandatory | [C / PC / NC]
TR-024 | General — ServiceNow Integration | Mandatory | [C / PC / NC]
TR-025 | General — Reporting | Mandatory | [C / PC / NC]

Procurement Milestone | Target Date | Notes
RFP Issue & Publication | 10 October 2025 | Issued via Apex procurement portal to pre-qualified vendors
Vendor Registration Deadline | 17 October 2025 | Registration via procurement portal only. Email registration not accepted.
Pre-Bid Conference (Virtual) | 22 October 2025, 11:00 IST | Microsoft Teams. Invitation issued to all registered vendors. Attendance mandatory for pre-qualification.
Vendor Written Q&A Submission Deadline | 31 October 2025 | Via procurement portal Q&A module only. Email queries not accepted.
Apex Q&A Response Publication | 10 November 2025 | Published simultaneously to all registered vendors. Responses form binding amendments to this RFP.
Final Proposal Submission Deadline | 21 November 2025, 17:00 IST | Via procurement portal. Late submissions automatically disqualified.
Technical Evaluation & Scoring | 21 Nov — 05 Dec 2025 | Internal evaluation by Apex Technical Evaluation Committee
Shortlist Notification (Top 3 Vendors) | 08 December 2025 | Written notification via procurement portal
Vendor Technical Demonstrations / PoC | 15–19 December 2025 | Apex HQ, Bhiwandi. Scenario brief issued 5 business days prior.
Best and Final Offer (BAFO) | 09 January 2026 | Invited shortlisted vendors only
Contract Award Notification | 23 January 2026 | Formal written notification. Unsuccessful vendors notified simultaneously.
Contract Execution and Programme Kick-Off | 03 February 2026 | Programme kick-off meeting at Apex HQ, Bhiwandi

Phase | Title | Scope | Site Count | Target Duration
Ph-1 | Foundation & Pilot | SD-WAN CPE design lock; Mumbai DC cloud interconnect build; 5 pilot hub sites; SOC onboarding and SIEM integration | 5 pilot sites + DC | Months 1–3
Ph-2 | Hub Rollout | All 12 Tier-1 hub sites migrated; active-active dual-link SD-WAN live; NGFW policy baseline deployed | 12 sites | Months 4–6

Phase | Description | Activities | Sites | Timeline
Ph-3 | Branch Rollout — North & West | First 100 Tier-2/Tier-3 branch sites (North and West India zones) | 100 sites | Months 7–11
Ph-4 | Branch Rollout — South & East | Remaining 128 Tier-2/Tier-3 branch sites (South and East India zones) | 128 sites | Months 12–16
Ph-5 | Steady-State & Handover | MPLS decommissioning confirmation; IoT SIM migration completion; SOC BAU handover; acceptance testing; documentation finalisation | All sites | Months 17–18

Requirement | Specification
Format | Single consolidated PDF/A (ISO 19005 compliant) containing the complete proposal, including all appendices and supporting documents.
Language | English only. All technical specifications, SLA commitments, and commercial terms must be in English.
Page Limit | Main body (Sections 1–12): Maximum 120 pages (A4, minimum 11pt Arial, 25mm margins). Appendices are not subject to a page limit.
Section Order | Proposals must follow the identical section numbering as this RFP. Reordering of sections is not permitted.
Requirements Table | The requirements matrix (Section 5.2) must be reproduced in its entirety with Vendor Response and Vendor Remarks columns completed. Requirement IDs must not be altered.
Pricing | Pricing must be included within the same consolidated document in a clearly marked section titled "COMMERCIAL PROPOSAL — STRICTLY CONFIDENTIAL", placed at the end of the document.
Deviations | Any deviations from this RFP’s requirements or proposed contractual terms must be compiled in a single 'Deviations Register' table as Appendix G. Deviations embedded in the main proposal text without corresponding Appendix G entry will be disregarded.

Appendix | Content Required
Appendix A | Vendor Financials — Audited annual financial statements for FY2022–23, FY2023–24, and FY2024–25
Appendix B | Certifications — ISO 27001:2022, ISO 20000-1:2018, SOC 2 Type II report, PCI-DSS AoC (if applicable), Microsoft Azure ExpressRoute Partner certificate, AWS Direct Connect Partner certificate
Appendix C | Case Studies — Minimum 2 detailed Tier-1 enterprise case studies in logistics or manufacturing (format per Section 8.3)

# | Criterion | Description | Evidence Required
1 | Annual Revenue | Vendor's consolidated annual revenue must exceed INR 500 Crore (or USD 60M equivalent) in each of the last three completed financial years. | Audited financial statements FY22–23, FY23–24, FY24–25
2 | ISO 27001:2022 Certification | Vendor and all subcontractors with >20% of delivery scope must hold a current, in-scope ISO/IEC 27001:2022 certification from an accredited body. | Current certificates — Appendix B
3 | ISO 20000-1:2018 Certification | Vendor must hold ISO/IEC 20000-1:2018 (IT Service Management) certification demonstrating ITIL-aligned service delivery capability. | Current certificates — Appendix B
4 | SOC 2 Type II Report | Vendor must provide a SOC 2 Type II report (not older than 12 months) covering Security, Availability, and Confidentiality Trust Service Criteria for all cloud-managed service components. | SOC 2 Type II Report — Appendix B
5 | India Operations | Vendor must maintain a wholly owned or majority-controlled legal entity registered in India, with a minimum 24x7 NOC and SOC staffed by a minimum of 50 India-based FTEs. | CIN certificate + India staffing declaration
6 | Cloud Partner Designations | Vendor must hold active Microsoft Azure ExpressRoute Partner AND AWS Direct Connect Delivery Partner designations at the time of proposal submission. | Partner designation certificates — Appendix B
7 | Comparable Deployments | Vendor must demonstrate a minimum of two (2) completed Managed SD-WAN deployments covering 100+ sites each in India, within the last four years, in the logistics, manufacturing, or BFSI sector. | Case Studies — Appendix C (format per Section 8.3)
8 | No Material Sanctions | Vendor, its subsidiaries, and key subcontractors must not be subject to any material economic sanction, debartment from Government of India procurement, or CBI/ED enforcement action. | Self-certification by authorised signatory

Role | Minimum Qualifications | Key Responsibilities
Programme Director | 15+ years' experience; minimum 3 as Programme Director for network | Overall delivery governance; executive escalation; contractual accountability;

Role | Requirements | Responsibilities
Chief Network Architect | 10+ years in SD-WAN / MPLS / cloud networking; Cisco CCIE or equivalent; verifiable Azure/AWS networking certifications. | End-to-end SD-WAN and cloud interconnect architecture design, compliance verification, and integration governance.
Head of Delivery (Project Manager) | PMP or PRINCE2 Practitioner certification mandatory; 7+ years managing large multi-site network rollouts. | Day-to-day delivery management; site migration scheduling; milestone tracking; RAID log management.
SOC Operations Lead | CISSP or CISM mandatory; 5+ years SOC operations management; demonstrable SIEM administration experience. | SOC operations governance; threat detection tuning; incident response escalation; monthly reporting.
IoT / Mobility Lead | 5+ years IoT/EMM platform management; GSMA eSIM certification preferred; telco SIM management background. | IoT SIM platform migration; EMM policy design; private APN architecture; API integration.

Evaluation Dimension | Weight | Sub-Criteria
Technical Capability & Solution Quality | 40% | • Requirements matrix compliance (TR-001 to TR-025) — 20%
• SD-WAN solution architecture quality & OEM credentials — 8%
• Cloud interconnect design & partner credentials — 6%
• Migration plan quality (phasing, ZTP, risk management) — 6%
Commercial Competitiveness | 30% | • Total Cost of Ownership (TCO) over 36-month contract term — 18%
• OpEx hardware-as-a-service pricing model quality — 6%
• Commercial flexibility (payment milestones, volume discounts) — 6%
Security, Compliance & SOC Quality | 20% | • ISO 27001, ISO 20000-1, SOC 2 Type II currency and scope — 8%
• SOC maturity: SIEM capability, analyst headcount, MT TD / MTTR evidence — 8%
• DPDP Act compliance framework & data localisation architecture — 4%
References & Track Record | 10% | • Quality and relevance of case studies (Section 8.3) — 6%
• Verified client reference calls (shortlisted vendors only) — 4%
TOTAL | 100% | Minimum qualifying score to proceed to BAFO: 65 out of 100

Score | Rating | Interpretation
5 | Excellent | Exceeds requirement with demonstrated evidence and innovation
4 | Good | Fully meets requirement with strong evidence

Score | Compliance Level | Description
3 | Adequate | Meets requirement with acceptable evidence
2 | Marginal | Partially meets requirement; material gaps identified
1 | Poor | Significant deficiencies; does not meaningfully satisfy requirement
0 | Non-Compliant | No response provided or requirement explicitly not met

Line # | Item Description | Category | Unit Type | NRC (One-Time) INR | MRC / Annual INR

RFP Reference: AILL-PROC-2025-0078  |  Issuer: Apex Industrial Logistics Limited  |  Issue Date: 10 October 2025
© 2025 Apex Industrial Logistics Limited. All Rights Reserved. Unauthorised reproduction or distribution is prohibited.

Pain Point | Current Impact on Operations
Legacy MPLS Network | All 300 branch warehouses are connected via legacy Multiprotocol Label Switching (MPLS) circuits procured from a single incumbent carrier. Bandwidth tiers are rigid (2 Mbps to 10 Mbps per branch), costly to upgrade, and require 60–90-day lead times for any configuration change.
Chronic Network Outages | The Company experienced an average of 4.3 unplanned network outages per month per region in FY2024–25, each lasting an average of 47 minutes. These outages directly halt warehouse management system (WMS) operations, fleet tracking updates, and ERP transaction processing, resulting in an estimated revenue impact of INR 3.8 Crore per month.
Single-Link Dependency | Hub warehouse sites (Tier-1) currently operate on a single MPLS link with no active failover path. Loss of connectivity at a hub site cascades to 8–15 dependent spoke sites reliant on the hub for WMS access and IoT aggregation.
No Cloud On-Ramp | Apex is executing a cloud-first transformation programme (ERP on Microsoft Azure, data analytics on AWS). Currently, all cloud traffic is backhauled through the Mumbai Data Centre, introducing latency of 180–340 ms for branch-to-cloud workloads and consuming disproportionate Data Centre bandwidth.
Security Perimeter Gaps | Branch sites lack next-generation firewall (NGFW) protection. A significant ransomware incident in Q3 FY2024 resulted in operational downtime of 72 hours across 14 warehouse sites and a forensic investigation cost of INR 1.1 Crore. Apex currently has no 24/7 Security Operations Centre (SOC) capability.
IoT Connectivity Fragmentation | Apex operates a fleet of 2.5 million IoT-enabled tracking SIMs across its vehicle fleet and warehouse assets. SIM Management is currently distributed across three different Mobile Virtual Network Operators (MVNOs), resulting in fragmented visibility, inconsistent data plans, and inability to enforce unified MDM policies.

Req. ID | Category | Description | Priority | Vendor Response | Vendor Remarks
TR-001 | SD-WAN — Architecture | The SD-WAN solution MUST be a cloud-managed, fully software-defined overlay capable of supporting all 300 Apex branch sites within a single management domain without requiring a hardware controller at the Data Centre. | Mandatory | [C / PC / NC] | [Vendor to fill]
TR-002 | SD-WAN — ZTP | Zero-Touch Provisioning (ZTP) MUST be supported for all CPE devices. A branch site MUST be fully operational within 4 hours of CPE delivery, without requiring an on-site engineer from the vendor. | Mandatory | [C / PC / NC] | [Vendor to fill]
TR-003 | SD-WAN — Failover | Dynamic path failover from primary to secondary link MUST complete in under 50 milliseconds as measured end-to-end from packet loss detection to traffic rerouting, without manual intervention. | Mandatory | [C / PC / NC] | [Vendor to fill]
TR-004 | SD-WAN — BGP | The solution MUST support BGP routing with automated route advertisement and withdrawal upon link failure, supporting integration with Apex's Mumbai DC edge router (Cisco ASR 1002-HX) via eBGP peering. | Mandatory | [C / PC / NC] | [Vendor to fill]

TR-ID | Requirement | Description | Compliance | Vendor Response
TR-005 | SD-WAN — OEM Hardware | Vendor MUST specify the OEM hardware partner(s) for SD-WAN CPE (e.g., Cisco, Fortinet, VMware Velocloud, Palo Alto). CPE must be provided as-a-Service under OpEx pricing. Vendor-owned hardware is mandatory; Apex will not purchase CPE outright. | Mandatory | [C / PC / NC] [Vendor to fill — Name OEM]
TR-006 | SD-WAN — QoS | The solution MUST support a minimum of 8 QoS traffic classes with guaranteed bandwidth (GBW) and maximum bandwidth (MBW) policies configurable per application, per site. Guaranteed bandwidth for SAP S/4HANA and WMS traffic must be configurable independently of other traffic classes. | Mandatory | [C / PC / NC] [Vendor to fill]
TR-007 | SD-WAN — Security | Integrated NGFW capability at the SD-WAN edge MUST include IPS/IDS, URL filtering (minimum 60 URL categories), DNS security, and SSL deep inspection. Vendor must specify the OEM firewall solution (e.g., Fortinet FortiGate, Palo Alto Networks NGFW, Check Point). | Mandatory | [C / PC / NC] [Vendor to fill — Name Firewall OEM]
TR-008 | SD-WAN — Portal | A self-service portal with role-based access control (RBAC) MUST be provided to Apex IT staff with read-only visibility of network topology, link health, traffic statistics, and active alerts across all 300 sites. No additional licensing cost for portal access. | Mandatory | [C / PC / NC] [Vendor to fill]
TR-009 | SD-WAN — Project Mgmt | Vendor MUST provide a detailed 300-site migration project plan conforming to either PRINCE2 or PMBOK methodology. A dedicated Project Manager (minimum PMP or PRINCE2 Practitioner certified) must be named in the proposal and committed to the programme through completion. | Mandatory | [C / PC / NC] [Vendor to fill]
TR-010 | Cloud Interconnect — Azure | Vendor MUST hold an active Microsoft Azure ExpressRoute Partner designation and provide two (2) x 10 Gbps circuits in active-active configuration to the Azure Central India region. Evidence of Microsoft partner designation MUST be attached as Appendix B. | Mandatory | [C / PC / NC] [Vendor to fill]
TR-011 | Cloud Interconnect — AWS | Vendor MUST hold an active AWS Direct Connect Delivery Partner designation and provide one (1) x 10 Gbps Dedicated Connection to the AWS ap-south-1 Mumbai region. Evidence of AWS partner designation MUST be attached as Appendix B. | Mandatory | [C / PC / NC] [Vendor to fill]

TR-ID | Requirement | Description | Compliance Status | Vendor Response
TR-012 | Cloud Interconnect — Latency | The Azure ExpressRoute interconnect MUST achieve a maximum round-trip latency of 8 ms between the Apex Mumbai DC and the Azure Central India region under steady-state traffic conditions. Vendor must provide benchmark test evidence from equivalent deployments. | Mandatory | [C / PC / NC] [Vendor to fill]
TR-013 | SOC — Availability | The Managed SOC service MUST operate 24 hours per day, 7 days per week, 365 days per year (including Indian public holidays) with no scheduled maintenance windows that reduce monitoring coverage below Tier-1 analyst staffing levels. | Mandatory | [C / PC / NC] [Vendor to fill]
TR-014 | SOC — SIEM Platform | The SIEM platform used by the SOC MUST ingest logs from all 300 SD-WAN edge devices, the Mumbai DC, and cloud interconnects. The vendor must name the SIEM product proposed (e.g., Microsoft Sentinel, Splunk Enterprise Security, IBM QRadar, or equivalent). SIEM data MUST be stored in India. | Mandatory | [C / PC / NC] [Vendor to fill — Name SIEM]
TR-015 | SOC — Incident Response SLAs | P1 incidents MUST be acknowledged within 15 minutes and contained within 4 hours. P2 incidents MUST be acknowledged within 30 minutes and resolved within 8 hours. P3 incidents within 2 hours acknowledgement and 24 hours resolution. All timings measured 24x7. | Mandatory | [C / PC / NC] [Vendor to fill]
TR-016 | SOC — ITIL Alignment | The SOC MUST operate Incident Management, Problem Management, and Change Management processes conforming to ITIL v4. Root Cause Analysis (RCA) reports for P1 and P2 incidents MUST be delivered within 72 hours of incident containment. Vendor must provide sample RCA template with proposal. | Mandatory | [C / PC / NC] [Vendor to fill]
TR-017 | SOC — Threat Intelligence | Vendor MUST demonstrate active subscriptions to a minimum of two (2) commercial Threat Intelligence platforms and provide evidence of integration with the proposed SIEM, including IOC ingestion frequency and automated blocking workflow. | Mandatory | [C / PC / NC] [Vendor to fill]
TR-018 | Compliance — DPDP Act 2023 | Vendor MUST provide a written Data Privacy Impact Assessment (DPIA) framework for the proposed solution, demonstrating how personal data of Apex employees and customers processed across the network is handled in compliance with the Digital Personal Data Protection | Mandatory | [C / PC / NC] [Vendor to fill]

Requirement | Specification
Format | Single consolidated PDF/A (ISO 19005 compliant) containing the complete proposal, including all appendices and supporting documents.
Language | English only. All technical specifications, SLA commitments, and commercial terms must be in English.
Page Limit | Main body (Sections 1–12): Maximum 120 pages (A4, minimum 11pt Arial, 25mm margins). Appendices are not subject to a page limit.
Section Order | Proposals must follow the identical section numbering as this RFP. Reordering of sections is not permitted.
Requirements Table | The requirements matrix (Section 5.2) must be reproduced in its entirety with Vendor Response and Vendor Remarks columns completed. Requirement IDs must not be altered.
Pricing | Pricing must be included within the same consolidated document in a clearly marked section titled "COMMERCIAL PROPOSAL — STRICTLY CONFIDENTIAL", placed at the end of the document.
Deviations | Any deviations from this RFP’s requirements or proposed contractual terms must be compiled in a single 'Deviations Register' table as Appendix G. Deviations embedded in the main proposal text without corresponding Appendix G entry will be disregarded.

Appendix | Content Required
Appendix A | Vendor Financials — Audited annual financial statements for FY2022–23, FY2023–24, and FY2024–25
Appendix B | Certifications — ISO 27001:2022, ISO 20000-1:2018, SOC 2 Type II report, PCI-DSS AoC (if applicable), Microsoft Azure ExpressRoute Partner certificate, AWS Direct Connect Partner certificate
Appendix C | Case Studies — Minimum 2 detailed Tier-1 enterprise case studies in logistics or manufacturing (format per Section 8.3)

# | Criterion | Description | Evidence Required
1 | Annual Revenue | Vendor's consolidated annual revenue must exceed INR 500 Crore (or USD 60M equivalent) in each of the last three completed financial years. | Audited financial statements FY22–23, FY23–24, FY24–25
2 | ISO 27001:2022 Certification | Vendor and all subcontractors with >20% of delivery scope must hold a current, in-scope ISO/IEC 27001:2022 certification from an accredited body. | Current certificates — Appendix B
3 | ISO 20000-1:2018 Certification | Vendor must hold ISO/IEC 20000-1:2018 (IT Service Management) certification demonstrating ITIL-aligned service delivery capability. | Current certificates — Appendix B
4 | SOC 2 Type II Report | Vendor must provide a SOC 2 Type II report (not older than 12 months) covering Security, Availability, and Confidentiality Trust Service Criteria for all cloud-managed service components. | SOC 2 Type II Report — Appendix B
5 | India Operations | Vendor must maintain a wholly owned or majority-controlled legal entity registered in India, with a minimum 24x7 NOC and SOC staffed by a minimum of 50 India-based FTEs. | CIN certificate + India staffing declaration
6 | Cloud Partner Designations | Vendor must hold active Microsoft Azure ExpressRoute Partner AND AWS Direct Connect Delivery Partner designations at the time of proposal submission. | Partner designation certificates — Appendix B
7 | Comparable Deployments | Vendor must demonstrate a minimum of two (2) completed Managed SD-WAN deployments covering 100+ sites each in India, within the last four years, in the logistics, manufacturing, or BFSI sector. | Case Studies — Appendix C (format per Section 8.3)
8 | No Material Sanctions | Vendor, its subsidiaries, and key subcontractors must not be subject to any material economic sanction, debartment from Government of India procurement, or CBI/ED enforcement action. | Self-certification by authorised signatory

response_sections.title: Cover Letter
response_sections.full_text:
Dear Apex Industrial Logistics Team,

We are pleased to submit our proposal in response to RFP AILL-PROC-2025-0078 for industrial logistics services. This proposal is submitted by Vodafone Business, and we confirm that it is our intention to provide a comprehensive solution that meets your requirements.

We acknowledge that our proposal must be valid for a period of ninety (90) days from the submission date. Our proposal is structured in accordance with the requirements outlined in the RFP, and we confirm that all information provided is accurate and up-to-date.

We understand that the submission deadline for this RFP is 21 November 2025, 17:00 IST, and we confirm that our proposal is being submitted on time. We also acknowledge that there may be no conflict of interest in our submission, and we declare that our proposal is made in good faith.

We appreciate the opportunity to work with Apex Industrial Logistics and look forward to the opportunity to discuss our proposal in further detail. Our authorized signatory has reviewed and approved this proposal.

Please find attached our comprehensive proposal, which includes all required sections and supporting documentation. We confirm that our proposal is confidential and should be treated as such.

Thank you for considering our proposal.

Sincerely,
[Your Name]
Vodafone Business
